You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 18→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19→
 20→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→
 26→const val PORT = 0
 27→// const val PORT = 40001
 28→
 29→// local debugging:
 30→// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31→
 32→open class AnkiServer(
 33→    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34→) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35→
 36→    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37→        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41→    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42→    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43→
 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        return when (session.method) {
 46→            Method.POST -> {
 47→                val uri = session.uri
 48→                Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 49→                val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 50→                buildResponse {
 51→                    postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 52→                }
 53→            }
 54→            Method.GET -> newFixedLengthResponse(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND, null, null)
 55→            else -> newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun buildResponse(
 60→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 61→    ): Response {
 62→        return try {
 63→            val data = runBlocking {
 64→                block()
 65→            }
 66→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 67→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 68→            Timber.w(exc, "buildResponse failure")
 69→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 70→        }
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    companion object {
 74→        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 75→
 76→        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 77→        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 78→        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 79→
 80→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 81→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 82→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 83→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 84→            return bytes
 85→        }
 86→
 87→        fun newChunkedResponse(
 88→            data: ByteArray?,
 89→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
 90→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
 91→        ): Response {
 92→            return if (data == null) {
 93→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 94→            } else {
 95→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
 96→            }
 97→        }
 98→    }
 99→}

[thinking: Now I understand the issue better. The `WebResourceResponse` constructor can take additional parameters for response headers. Let me look at the available constructors for `WebResourceResponse`.

The ...]
Now I understand the issue. The problem is that when an SVG is loaded via `<object>` tag, the JavaScript can't access `contentDocument` because of same-origin policy. Even though the SVG is served from the same domain (`127.0.0.1:port`), the browser security model for embedded objects requires CORS headers to allow JavaScript access.

The fix is to add CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin`) to the responses in `ViewerAssetLoader.kt`. Let me also check the CardViewerFragment to ensure the fix is consistent across all viewers.

 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)

 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }

121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

package com.ichi2.anki

import android.content.Context
import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
import timber.log.Timber
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
import java.io.File
import java.io.FileInputStream

fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
        .setHttpAllowed(true)
        .setDomain(domain)
        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
            }
            try {
                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
            } catch (e: Exception) {
                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
                null
            }
        }
        .build()

package com.ichi2.anki

import android.content.Context
import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
import timber.log.Timber
import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
import java.io.File
import java.io.FileInputStream

/**
 * CORS headers to allow JavaScript to access content of embedded objects (e.g., SVG files
 * loaded via <object> tags). Without these headers, contentDocument access would be blocked
 * by the browser's same-origin policy.
 * See: https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15522
 */
private val CORS_HEADERS = mapOf(
    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" to "*"
)

fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
        .setHttpAllowed(true)
        .setDomain(domain)
        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
            }
            try {
                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
                WebResourceResponse(
                    guessMimeType(path),
                    null,
                    HTTP_OK,
                    HTTP_OK_REASON_PHRASE,
                    CORS_HEADERS,
                    inputStream
                )
            } catch (e: Exception) {
                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
                null
            }
        }
        .build()
}

private const val HTTP_OK = 200
private const val HTTP_OK_REASON_PHRASE = "OK""}

[thinking: The test file exists but it doesn't directly test the asset loader. The fix I made is a simple and targeted change that adds CORS headers to the response. The change is minimal and doesn't affect exis...]
The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
SVG files loaded via `<object>` tags in card templates couldn't be accessed by JavaScript due to same-origin policy restrictions. When the JavaScript code tried to access `img_obj.contentDocument.querySelectorAll()`, it returned `null` because the browser blocked cross-origin access to the embedded document.

### Root Cause
The `WebResourceResponse` returned by `getViewerAssetLoader` didn't include CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) headers. Even though the content was being served from the same domain (`127.0.0.1:port`), the browser's security model for embedded objects (`<object>`, `<iframe>`, etc.) requires CORS headers to allow JavaScript access to their `contentDocument`.

### Fix
Modified `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` to:

1. **Added CORS headers** - Created a `CORS_HEADERS` map containing `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` 
2. **Updated `WebResourceResponse` constructor** - Changed from the simple 3-argument constructor to the 6-argument constructor that accepts response headers
3. **Added documentation** - Included a KDoc comment explaining why CORS headers are needed with a reference to the GitHub issue

### Files Affected by This Fix
The `getViewerAssetLoader` function is used in two places:
- `AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - The main card reviewer 
- `CardViewerFragment.kt` - The card previewer

Both will now correctly serve media files with CORS headers, allowing JavaScript in card templates to access `contentDocument` of embedded SVG files.

 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)

[thinking: Looking at the code, I can see the issue. In `HeaderFragment.kt`, the `configureSearchBar` function sets up the search configuration and indexes various preference XML files. However, I notice that `R...]
Now I can see the issue. In the `HeaderFragment.kt` file, the `configureSearchBar` function indexes various preference XML files for the search feature, but `R.xml.preferences_backup_limits` is NOT included in the list of indexed files. This is why searching for "back" doesn't show "Backups" and its sub-options.

Let me also check if there are similar patterns for other preference screens and see the preference headers file:

 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
125→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
126→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
128→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
129→            }
130→
131→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
132→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
133→
134→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
135→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
136→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
137→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
138→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
139→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
140→                }
141→            }
142→
143→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
144→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
145→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
146→            }
147→
148→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
149→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
151→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
152→            }
153→
154→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
155→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
156→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))
157→            }
158→
159→            searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.user_actions_controls_category_key))
160→        }
161→    }
162→}

The fix is complete. Let me summarize the change:

## Summary

**Root Cause:** The `configureSearchBar` function in `HeaderFragment.kt` indexes various preference XML files for the Settings search feature. However, `R.xml.preferences_backup_limits` was NOT included in the list of indexed files. This is why searching for "back" or "backup" did not show the "Backups" options and its sub-options.

**Solution:** Added `index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)` to the search configuration in the `configureSearchBar` function (line 123).

**Change Made:**
```kotlin
// Before:
index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)

// After:
index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)  // Added this line
index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
```

This minimal, targeted change ensures that the "Backups" settings screen and all its sub-options (like "Minutes between automatic backups", "Daily backups to keep", "Weekly backups to keep", and "Monthly backups to keep") will now appear in the search results when users search for terms like "back", "backup", etc.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 6b1ba50b).

Group commands by categories
Currently we have 39 possible commands in the control preferences (if I counted them right), which is a big number. 

Besides searching, to find a command more easily, I suggest grouping them in some categories, like:

- Answer buttons

    - Answer button 1
    - Answer button 2
    - ...
- Flags
    - red
    - blue
    - orange
    - ...
- Whiteboard

- Current card/note

    - delete

    - ...
- Navigation

- Misc

 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→

123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(

138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }
172→
173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }
188→
189→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
190→        /**
191→         *
192→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
193→         */
194→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
195→    }
196→}
197→

18→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory

20→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
21→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
22→import com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference
23→
24→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
25→    override val preferenceResource: Int
26→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
27→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
28→        get() = "prefs.controls"
29→
30→    override fun initSubscreen() {
31→        val commandMappingCategory = requirePreference<PreferenceCategory>(R.string.controls_command_mapping_cat_key)
32→        addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(commandMappingCategory)
33→    }
34→
35→    /** Attaches all possible [ControlPreference] elements to a given [PreferenceCategory] */
36→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToCategory(category: PreferenceCategory) {
37→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
38→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
39→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
40→                key = command.preferenceKey
41→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
42→            }
43→            category.addPreference(preference)
44→        }
45→    }
46→}

 17→package com.ichi2.preferences
 18→
 19→import android.annotation.SuppressLint
 20→import android.content.Context
 21→import android.util.AttributeSet
 22→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 23→import androidx.preference.ListPreference
 24→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.MaterialDialog
 25→import com.afollestad.materialdialogs.customview.customView
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.UIUtils
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.GestureProcessor
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.CardSideSelectionDialog
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.GestureSelectionDialogUtils.onGestureChanged
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.KeySelectionDialogUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 36→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromGesture
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 39→import com.ichi2.ui.KeyPicker
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 41→import java.util.*
 42→
 43→/**
 44→ * A preference which allows mapping of inputs to actions (example: keys -> commands)
 45→ *
 46→ * This is implemented as a List, the elements allow the user to either add, or
 47→ * remove previously mapped keys
 48→ *
 49→ * Future:
 50→ * * Allow mapping gestures here
 51→ * * Allow maps other than the reviewer
 52→ */
 53→class ControlPreference : ListPreference {
 54→    @Suppress("unused")
 55→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int, defStyleRes: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr, defStyleRes)
 56→
 57→    @Suppress("unused")
 58→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?, defStyleAttr: Int) : super(context, attrs, defStyleAttr)
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("unused")
 61→    constructor(context: Context, attrs: AttributeSet?) : super(context, attrs)
 62→
 63→    @Suppress("unused")
 64→    constructor(context: Context) : super(context)
 65→
 66→    fun refreshEntries() {
 67→        val entryTitles: MutableList<CharSequence> = ArrayList()
 68→        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 69→        // negative indices are "add"
 70→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 71→        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 72→        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 73→        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 74→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 75→            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 76→        }
 77→        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 78→        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 79→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 80→            entryIndices.add(i)
 81→        }
 82→        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 83→        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun onClick() {
 87→        refreshEntries()
 88→        super.onClick()
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 92→    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
 93→        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
 94→
 95→    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
 96→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
 97→    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
 98→        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
 99→            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
100→                val actionName = title
101→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
102→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
103→
104→                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
105→
106→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { materialDialog ->
107→                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
108→                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(gesture)
109→                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
110→                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), materialDialog)
111→                        } else {
112→                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
113→                            dismiss()
114→                        }
115→                    }
116→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
117→                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
118→
119→                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
120→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(fromGesture(gesture))
121→                    }
122→
123→                    noAutoDismiss()
124→                }
125→            }
126→            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
127→                val actionName = title
128→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
129→                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
130→                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
131→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
132→
133→                    // When the user presses a key
134→                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
135→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH)))
136→                    }
137→
138→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
139→                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
140→                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
141→                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
142→                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
143→                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
144→                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
145→                            } else {
146→                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
147→                                dismiss()
148→                            }
149→                        }
150→                    }
151→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
152→
153→                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
154→                    noAutoDismiss()
155→                }
156→            }
157→            else -> {
158→                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
159→                bindings.removeAt(index)
160→                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
161→            }
162→        }
163→        // don't persist the value
164→        return false
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Return if another command uses
169→     */
170→    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
171→        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
172→    }
173→
174→    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
175→    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
176→        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
177→            ?: return
178→
179→        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
180→        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
181→        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
182→    }
183→
184→    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
185→    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
186→        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
187→            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
188→            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
189→    }
190→
191→    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
192→        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
193→        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
194→        bindings.remove(binding)
195→        bindings.add(0, binding)
196→        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Remove binding from all control preferences
201→     * in the parent PreferenceGroup of this [ControlPreference]
202→     */
203→    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
204→        val preferenceGroup = parent
205→            ?: return
206→
207→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
208→            val commandPreference = preferenceGroup.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)!!
209→            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
210→            if (binding in bindings) {
211→                bindings.remove(binding)
212→                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
213→            }
214→        }
215→    }
216→
217→    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: MaterialDialog) {
218→        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
219→

222→            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
223→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
224→                clearBinding(binding)
225→                addBinding(binding)
226→                parentDialog.dismiss()
227→            }
228→            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
229→        }
230→    }

 68→        val entryIndices: MutableList<Int> = ArrayList()
 69→        // negative indices are "add"
 70→        entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_key))
 71→        entryIndices.add(ADD_KEY_INDEX)
 72→        // Put "Add gesture" option if gestures are enabled
 73→        if (context.sharedPrefs().getBoolean(GestureProcessor.PREF_KEY, false)) {
 74→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_add_gesture))
 75→            entryIndices.add(ADD_GESTURE_INDEX)
 76→        }
 77→        // 0 and above are "delete" actions for already mapped preferences
 78→        for ((i, binding) in MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value).withIndex()) {
 79→            entryTitles.add(context.getString(R.string.binding_remove_binding, binding.toDisplayString(context)))
 80→            entryIndices.add(i)
 81→        }
 82→        entries = entryTitles.toTypedArray()
 83→        entryValues = entryIndices.map { it.toString() }.toTypedArray()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    override fun onClick() {
 87→        refreshEntries()
 88→        super.onClick()
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    /** The summary that appears on the preference */
 92→    override fun getSummary(): CharSequence = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
 93→        .joinToString(", ") { it.toDisplayString(context) }
 94→
 95→    /** Called when an element is selected in the ListView */
 96→    @SuppressLint("CheckResult")
 97→    override fun callChangeListener(newValue: Any?): Boolean {
 98→        when (val index: Int = (newValue as String).toInt()) {
 99→            ADD_GESTURE_INDEX -> {
100→                val actionName = title
101→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
102→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
103→
104→                    val gesturePicker = GestureSelectionDialogUtils.getGesturePicker(context)
105→
106→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { materialDialog ->
107→                        val gesture = gesturePicker.getGesture() ?: return@positiveButton
108→                        val mappableBinding = fromGesture(gesture)
109→                        if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
110→                            showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_gesture), materialDialog)
111→                        } else {
112→                            addBinding(mappableBinding)
113→                            dismiss()
114→                        }
115→                    }
116→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
117→                    customView(view = gesturePicker)
118→
119→                    gesturePicker.onGestureChanged { gesture ->
120→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(fromGesture(gesture))
121→                    }
122→
123→                    noAutoDismiss()
124→                }
125→            }
126→            ADD_KEY_INDEX -> {
127→                val actionName = title
128→                MaterialDialog(context).show {
129→                    val keyPicker: KeyPicker = KeyPicker.inflate(context)
130→                    customView(view = keyPicker.rootLayout)
131→                    title(text = actionName.toString())
132→
133→                    // When the user presses a key
134→                    keyPicker.setBindingChangedListener { binding ->
135→                        showToastIfBindingIsUsed(MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(CardSide.BOTH)))
136→                    }
137→
138→                    positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) {
139→                        val binding = keyPicker.getBinding() ?: return@positiveButton
140→                        // Use CardSide.BOTH as placeholder just to check if binding exists
141→                        CardSideSelectionDialog.displayInstance(context) { side ->
142→                            val mappableBinding = MappableBinding(binding, MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
143→                            if (bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(mappableBinding)) {
144→                                showDialogToReplaceBinding(mappableBinding, context.getString(R.string.binding_replace_key), it)
145→                            } else {
146→                                addBinding(mappableBinding)
147→                                dismiss()
148→                            }
149→                        }
150→                    }
151→                    negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) { dismiss() }
152→
153→                    keyPicker.setKeycodeValidation(KeySelectionDialogUtils.disallowModifierKeyCodes())
154→                    noAutoDismiss()
155→                }
156→            }
157→            else -> {
158→                val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
159→                bindings.removeAt(index)
160→                value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
161→            }
162→        }
163→        // don't persist the value
164→        return false
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Return if another command uses
169→     */
170→    private fun bindingIsUsedOnAnotherCommand(binding: MappableBinding): Boolean {
171→        return getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding) != null
172→    }
173→
174→    /** Displays a warning to the user if the provided binding couldn't be used */
175→    private fun showToastIfBindingIsUsed(binding: MappableBinding) {
176→        val bindingCommand = getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)
177→            ?: return
178→
179→        val commandName = context.getString(bindingCommand.resourceId)
180→        val text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName)
181→        UIUtils.showThemedToast(context, text, true)
182→    }
183→
184→    /** @return command where the binding is mapped excluding the current command */
185→    private fun getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding: MappableBinding): ViewerCommand? {
186→        return MappableBinding.allMappings(context.sharedPrefs())
187→            // filter to the commands which have a binding matching this one except this
188→            .firstOrNull { x -> x.second.any { cmdBinding -> cmdBinding == binding } && x.first.preferenceKey != key }?.first
189→    }
190→
191→    private fun addBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
192→        val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(value)
193→        // by removing the binding, we ensure it's now at the start of the list
194→        bindings.remove(binding)
195→        bindings.add(0, binding)
196→        value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
197→    }
198→
199→    /**
200→     * Remove binding from all control preferences
201→     * in the parent PreferenceGroup of this [ControlPreference]
202→     */
203→    private fun clearBinding(binding: MappableBinding) {
204→        val preferenceGroup = parent
205→            ?: return
206→
207→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
208→            val commandPreference = preferenceGroup.findPreference<ControlPreference>(command.preferenceKey)!!
209→            val bindings = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(commandPreference.value)
210→            if (binding in bindings) {
211→                bindings.remove(binding)
212→                commandPreference.value = bindings.toPreferenceString()
213→            }
214→        }
215→    }
216→
217→    private fun showDialogToReplaceBinding(binding: MappableBinding, title: String, parentDialog: MaterialDialog) {
218→        val commandName = context.getString(getCommandWithBindingExceptThis(binding)!!.resourceId)
219→
220→        AlertDialog.Builder(context).show {
221→            title(text = title)
222→            message(text = context.getString(R.string.bindings_already_bound, commandName))
223→            positiveButton(R.string.dialog_positive_replace) {
224→                clearBinding(binding)
225→                addBinding(binding)
226→                parentDialog.dismiss()
227→            }
228→            negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel)
229→        }
230→    }
231→
232→    companion object {
233→        private const val ADD_KEY_INDEX = -2
234→        private const val ADD_GESTURE_INDEX = -1
235→    }
236→}
237→

 49→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.gesture_answer_1, CommandGroup.ANSWER),

 86→    companion object {
 87→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 88→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 89→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 90→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    val preferenceKey: String
 94→        get() = "binding_$name"
 95→
 96→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 97→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 98→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 99→            collection.remove(element)
100→            collection.add(0, element)
101→            true
102→        }
103→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
104→    }
105→
106→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
107→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
108→            // do not reorder the elements
109→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
110→                return@BiFunction false
111→            }
112→            collection.add(element)
113→            return@BiFunction true
114→        }
115→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
116→    }
117→
118→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
119→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
120→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
121→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
122→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
123→    }
124→
125→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
126→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
127→        bindings.remove(binding)
128→        prefs.edit {
129→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
130→        }
131→    }

133→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
134→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
135→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
136→            when (this) {
137→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
141→                )
142→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
144→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
145→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
146→                )
147→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
149→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
150→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
151→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
152→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
153→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
154→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
155→                )
156→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
157→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
158→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
159→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
160→                )
161→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
162→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
163→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
164→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
165→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
166→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
167→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
170→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
171→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
172→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
173→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
174→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
175→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
176→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
177→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
178→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
179→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
180→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
181→                else -> ArrayList()
182→            }
183→
184→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
185→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
186→    }
187→
188→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
189→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
193→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
194→    }
195→
196→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
197→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
198→    }
199→
200→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
201→        /**
202→         *
203→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
204→         */
205→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
206→    }
207→}

16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
17→
18→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
19→import androidx.preference.PreferenceScreen
20→import com.ichi2.anki.R
21→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.CommandGroup
22→import com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer.ViewerCommand
23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
24→import com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference
25→
26→class ControlsSettingsFragment : SettingsFragment() {
27→    override val preferenceResource: Int
28→        get() = R.xml.preferences_controls
29→    override val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
30→        get() = "prefs.controls"
31→
32→    override fun initSubscreen() {
33→        addAllControlPreferencesToScreen(preferenceScreen)
34→    }
35→
36→    /**
37→     * Creates [PreferenceCategory] elements for each [CommandGroup] and adds
38→     * all [ViewerCommand] elements as [ControlPreference] to their respective category
39→     */
40→    fun addAllControlPreferencesToScreen(screen: PreferenceScreen) {
41→        // Create a category for each command group
42→        val categoryMap = CommandGroup.values().associateWith { group ->
43→            PreferenceCategory(screen.context).apply {
44→                setTitle(group.resourceId)
45→                key = getCategoryKey(group)
46→                screen.addPreference(this)
47→            }
48→        }
49→
50→        // Add each command to its respective category
51→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
52→            val category = categoryMap[command.group] ?: continue
53→            val preference = ControlPreference(category.context).apply {
54→                setTitle(command.resourceId)
55→                key = command.preferenceKey
56→                setDefaultValue(command.defaultValue.toPreferenceString())
57→            }
58→            category.addPreference(preference)
59→        }
60→    }
61→
62→    companion object {
63→        /** Returns a unique key for the preference category based on [CommandGroup] */
64→        fun getCategoryKey(group: CommandGroup): String = "controls_category_${group.name.lowercase()}"
65→    }
66→}
67→

 27→import org.junit.Test

 30→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 31→class ControlsSettingsFragmentTest : RobolectricTest() {
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Ensures all [ViewerCommand] entries have a group assigned and are displayed in the UI.
 35→     * This test verifies that when new commands are added, they are properly categorized.
 36→     */
 37→    @Test
 38→    fun allCommandsHaveGroupsAssigned() {
 39→        // Verify all commands have a non-null group
 40→        for (command in ViewerCommand.values()) {
 41→            assertThat(
 42→                "Command ${command.name} should have a group assigned",
 43→                command.group in CommandGroup.values(),
 44→                equalTo(true)
 45→            )
 46→        }
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    /**
 50→     * Ensures all [ViewerCommand] entries are added to the preferences screen.
 51→     * This validates that the programmatic addition of commands covers all cases.
 52→     */
 53→    @Test
 54→    fun allCommandsAreAddedToPreferencesScreen() {
 55→        val activityScenario = ActivityScenario.launch(Preferences::class.java)
 56→
 57→        activityScenario.onActivity { activity ->
 58→            val fragment = ControlsSettingsFragment()
 59→            activity.supportFragmentManager.commitNow {
 60→                add(R.id.settings_container, fragment)
 61→            }
 62→
 63→            val screen = fragment.preferenceScreen
 64→            val allCommandKeys = ViewerCommand.values().map { it.preferenceKey }.toSet()
 65→            val foundKeys = mutableSetOf<String>()
 66→
 67→            // Search recursively through all preference groups to find control preferences
 68→            fun collectKeys(group: androidx.preference.PreferenceGroup) {
 69→                for (i in 0 until group.preferenceCount) {
 70→                    val pref = group.getPreference(i)
 71→                    if (pref is com.ichi2.preferences.ControlPreference) {
 72→                        foundKeys.add(pref.key)
 73→                    } else if (pref is androidx.preference.PreferenceGroup) {
 74→                        collectKeys(pref)
 75→                    }
 76→                }
 77→            }
 78→            collectKeys(screen)
 79→
 80→            val missingCommands = allCommandKeys - foundKeys
 81→            assertThat(
 82→                "All ViewerCommands should be present in the preferences screen. " +
 83→                    "Missing: $missingCommands",
 84→                missingCommands.isEmpty(),
 85→                equalTo(true)
 86→            )
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Ensures all [CommandGroup] categories are used by at least one command
 92→     */
 93→    @Test
 94→    fun allGroupsAreUsed() {
 95→        val usedGroups = ViewerCommand.values().map { it.group }.toSet()
 96→        val allGroups = CommandGroup.values().toSet()
 97→        val unusedGroups = allGroups - usedGroups
 98→
 99→        assertThat(
100→            "All CommandGroups should be used by at least one command. Unused: $unusedGroups",
101→            unusedGroups.isEmpty(),
102→            equalTo(true)
103→        )
104→    }
105→
106→    /**
107→     * Ensures category keys are correctly generated
108→     */
109→    @Test
110→    fun categoryKeysAreCorrectlyGenerated() {
111→        for (group in CommandGroup.values()) {
112→            val key = ControlsSettingsFragment.getCategoryKey(group)
113→            assertThat(
114→                "Category key for ${group.name} should follow naming convention",
115→                key,
116→                equalTo("controls_category_${group.name.lowercase()}")
117→            )
118→        }
119→    }
120→}

conversationHistory.content:
[thinking: These lint errors are pre-existing and not related to my changes. They are about attributes like `maxLength`, `comment`, etc. that are specific to the localization tools (Crowdin) and are intentionall...]
The lint errors shown for the XML files are pre-existing and are related to the localization tooling (Crowdin attributes like `maxLength` and `comment`). These are not issues introduced by my changes.

Let me provide a summary of all the changes I've made to resolve the issue:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. **ViewerCommand.kt** - Added CommandGroup enum and grouped commands
- Created a new `CommandGroup` enum with 5 categories: `ANSWER`, `FLAGS`, `CARD`, `WHITEBOARD`, `OTHER`
- Modified `ViewerCommand` enum to include a `group` property for each command
- Commands are now categorized as:
  - **Answer**: SHOW_ANSWER, FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1-4
  - **Flags**: All flag-related commands (red, orange, green, blue, pink, turquoise, purple, unset)
  - **Card/Note**: EDIT, MARK, BURY_CARD, BURY_NOTE, SUSPEND_CARD, SUSPEND_NOTE, DELETE, TAG, CARD_INFO, SHOW_HINT, SHOW_ALL_HINTS, RESCHEDULE_NOTE
  - **Whiteboard**: TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD, CLEAR_WHITEBOARD, CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR
  - **Other**: UNDO, PLAY_MEDIA, EXIT, PAGE_UP, PAGE_DOWN, ABORT_AND_SYNC, RECORD_VOICE, REPLAY_VOICE, ADD_NOTE

### 2. **ControlsSettingsFragment.kt** - Updated to create grouped categories
- Changed from adding all commands to a single category to creating a `PreferenceCategory` for each `CommandGroup`
- Commands are now added to their respective categories

### 3. **ControlPreference.kt** - Fixed clearBinding method
- Updated `clearBinding` to search from the `preferenceScreen` level instead of the direct parent, so it can find preferences across all categories

### 4. **Preferences.kt** - Updated search indexing
- Updated the breadcrumb for search results to use the command's group name instead of the old generic "Command mapping"

### 5. **preferences_controls.xml** - Removed old category
- Removed the old `PreferenceCategory` since categories are now created programmatically
- Added a comment explaining this

### 6. **10-preferences.xml** - Added new string resources
- Added string resources for the new category names:
  - `pref_cat_commands_answer` = "Answer"
  - `pref_cat_commands_flags` = "Flags"
  - `pref_cat_commands_card` = "Card / note"
  - `pref_cat_commands_other` = "Other"
- Reused existing `pref_cat_whiteboard` = "Whiteboard"

### 7. **ControlsSettingsFragmentTest.kt** - Added tests
- `allCommandsHaveGroupsAssigned()` - Ensures all commands have a valid group
- `allCommandsAreAddedToPreferencesScreen()` - Ensures all commands are present in the UI
- `allGroupsAreUsed()` - Ensures all groups have at least one command
- `categoryKeysAreCorrectlyGenerated()` - Verifies category key generation

This implementation:
- Groups the 37 commands into 5 logical categories for better organization
- Makes it easier to find commands in the preferences UI
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing preference keys
- Includes tests to ensure new commands added in the future are properly categorized

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1912 (commit: 1985077b).

## Issue to Resolve

 96→        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 97→    }

 99→    val preferenceKey: String
100→        get() = "binding_$name"
101→
102→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
103→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
104→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
105→            collection.remove(element)
106→            collection.add(0, element)
107→            true
108→        }
109→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
110→    }
111→
112→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
113→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
114→            // do not reorder the elements
115→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
116→                return@BiFunction false
117→            }
118→            collection.add(element)
119→            return@BiFunction true
120→        }
121→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
125→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
126→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
127→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
128→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
129→    }
130→
131→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
132→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
133→        bindings.remove(binding)
134→        prefs.edit {
135→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
136→        }
137→    }

160→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
161→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
162→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
163→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
164→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
165→                )
166→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
167→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
168→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
169→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
170→                )
171→                EDIT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
172→                MARK -> listOf(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
173→                BURY_CARD -> listOf(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
174→                BURY_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
175→                SUSPEND_CARD -> listOf(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
176→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
177→                PLAY_MEDIA -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
178→                REPLAY_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
179→                RECORD_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
180→                SAVE_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_S, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
181→                UNDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
182→                REDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH, ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = true, alt = false)))

184→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
185→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
186→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
187→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
188→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
189→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
190→                SHOW_HINT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))

198→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), screen)
