1→/****************************************************************************************
  2→ * Copyright (c) 2015 Timothy Rae <perceptualchaos2@gmail.com>                          *
  3→ *                                                                                      *
  4→ * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under        *
  5→ * the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software        *
  6→ * Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later           *
  7→ * version.                                                                             *
  8→ *                                                                                      *
  9→ * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY      *
 10→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A      *
 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 12→ *                                                                                      *
 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with         *
 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.Intent
 21→import android.net.Uri
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Message
 24→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26→import androidx.core.app.TaskStackBuilder
 27→import androidx.core.content.FileProvider
 28→import androidx.core.content.IntentCompat
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler.Companion.storeMessage
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.ScopedStorageService
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService
 34→import com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerFragment
 35→import com.ichi2.anki.worker.SyncWorker
 36→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 38→import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 39→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.handleFileImport
 40→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.isInvalidViewIntent
 41→import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil.resolveMimeType
 43→import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 44→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions
 45→import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions.hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission
 46→import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 47→import com.ichi2.utils.trimToLength
 48→import timber.log.Timber
 49→import java.io.File
 50→import kotlin.math.max
 51→import kotlin.math.min
 52→
 53→/**
 54→ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55→ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56→ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57→ *
 58→ * @author Tim
 59→ */
 60→class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62→        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64→        val intent = intent
 65→        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66→        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67→        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68→        val action = intent.action
 69→        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70→        // as this requires nothing
 71→        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72→        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73→        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74→        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75→            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76→        }
 77→        when (launchType) {
 78→            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80→                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81→                    finish()
 82→                }
 83→            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85→                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86→                    finish()
 87→                }
 88→            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89→                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90→                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91→                    finish()
 92→                }
 93→            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94→            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95→            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96→                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97→                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98→            }
 99→            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100→                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101→                finish()
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107→        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108→        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109→        this.copyToClipboard(
110→            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111→            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112→        )
113→    }
114→
115→    private val fileIntent: Intent
116→        get() {
117→            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119→            } else {
120→                intent
121→            }
122→        }
123→
124→    /**
125→     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126→     *
127→     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128→     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129→     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130→     *
131→     */
132→    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133→    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134→        reloadIntent: Intent,
135→        action: String?,
136→        block: () -> Unit,
137→    ) {
138→        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139→            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140→            block()
141→        } else {
142→            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143→            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148→        reloadIntent: Intent,
149→        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150→    ) {
151→        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152→        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153→
154→        val reviewIntent =
155→            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156→                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157→            } else {
158→                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159→                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160→                }
161→            }
162→        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163→        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164→        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165→        TaskStackBuilder
166→            .create(applicationContext)
167→            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168→            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169→            .startActivities()
170→        finish()
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174→        reloadIntent: Intent,
175→        action: String?,
176→    ) {
177→        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178→        sendDoSyncMsg()
179→        reloadIntent.action = action
180→        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181→        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182→        finish()
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun handleFileImport(
186→        intent: Intent,
187→        reloadIntent: Intent,
188→        action: String?,
189→    ) {
190→        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191→        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192→            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193→            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194→            return
195→        }
196→        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197→        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198→        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199→            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200→        }
201→
202→        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203→        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204→            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205→            reloadIntent.action = action
206→            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207→            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208→            finish()
209→        } else {
210→            Timber.i("File import failed")
211→            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212→            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213→        }
214→    }
215→
216→    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217→        try {
218→            val file = File(path!!)
219→            val fileUri =
220→                applicationContext?.let {
221→                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222→                        it,
223→                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224→                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225→                    )
226→                }
227→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228→            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229→            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230→        } catch (e: Exception) {
231→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232→        }
233→    }
234→
235→    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236→        val imageUri =
237→            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238→                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239→            } else {
240→                data.data
241→            }
242→
243→        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244→        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245→
246→        TaskStackBuilder
247→            .create(this)
248→            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249→            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250→            .startActivities()
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254→        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256→            return
257→        }
258→
259→        try {
260→            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261→            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262→            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263→        } catch (e: Exception) {
264→            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265→        }
266→    }
267→
268→    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269→        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270→        // otherwise go to previous task
271→        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272→        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273→        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274→        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275→        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276→        finish()
277→    }
278→
279→    // COULD_BE_BETTER: Also extract the parameters into here to reduce coupling
280→    @VisibleForTesting
281→    enum class LaunchType {
282→        DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
283→
284→        /** colpkg/apkg/unknown */
285→        FILE_IMPORT,
286→
287→        /** csv/tsv */
288→        TEXT_IMPORT,
289→
290→        /** image */
291→        IMAGE_IMPORT,
292→
293→        SYNC,
294→        REVIEW,
295→        COPY_DEBUG_INFO,
296→    }
297→
298→    companion object {
299→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT = "com.ichi2.anki.COPY_DEBUG_INFO"
300→        private const val CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA = "clip_data"
301→
302→        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303→            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304→            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305→            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306→        }
307→
308→        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309→         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310→         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311→         */
312→        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313→            context: Context,
314→            showToast: Boolean,
315→        ): Boolean {
316→            val granted =
317→                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318→                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319→                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320→
321→            if (!granted && showToast) {
322→                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323→            }
324→
325→            return granted
326→        }
327→
328→        @VisibleForTesting
329→        @CheckResult
330→        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331→            val action = intent.action
332→            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333→                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334→                when {
335→                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336→                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
337→                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
338→                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
339→                }
340→            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
341→                LaunchType.SYNC
342→            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
343→                LaunchType.REVIEW
344→            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
345→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
346→            } else {
347→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
348→            }
349→        }
350→
351→        /**
352→         * Send a Message to AnkiDroidApp so that the DialogMessageHandler forces a sync
353→         */
354→        fun sendDoSyncMsg() {
355→            // Store the message in AnkiDroidApp message holder, which is loaded later in AnkiActivity.onResume
356→            storeMessage(DoSync().toMessage())
357→        }
358→
359→        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
360→            context: Context,
361→            textToCopy: String,
362→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
363→            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT
364→            // max length for an intent is 500KB.
365→            // 25000 * 2 (bytes per char) = 50,000 bytes <<< 500KB
366→            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))
367→        }
368→
369→        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
370→            when (launchType) {
371→                LaunchType.SYNC,
372→                LaunchType.REVIEW,
373→                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
374→                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
375→                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
376→                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,
377→                -> true
378→                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> false
379→            }
380→
381→        class DoSync :
382→            DialogHandlerMessage(
383→                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
384→                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
385→            ) {
386→            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
387→                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
388→                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
389→                    showError(
390→                        activity,
391→                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
392→                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
393→                        true,
394→                    )
395→                    return
396→                }
397→                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
398→                val deckPicker = activity
399→
400→                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
401→                val res = deckPicker.resources
402→                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
403→                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
404→                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
405→                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
406→                    deckPicker.sync()
407→                } else {
408→                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
409→                    if (limited) {
410→                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
411→                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
412→                        // getQuantityString needs an int
413→                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
414→                        val message =
415→                            res.getQuantityString(
416→                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
417→                                remaining,
418→                                remaining,
419→                            )
420→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
421→                    } else {
422→                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
423→                            err,
424→                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
425→                            Channel.SYNC,
426→                        )
427→                    }
428→                }
429→                deckPicker.finish()
430→            }
431→
432→            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
433→
434→            companion object {
435→                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
436→                    (
437→                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
438→                    ).toLong()
439→            }
440→        }
441→
442→        /**
443→         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
444→         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
445→         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
446→         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
447→         */
448→        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
449→            context: Context,
450→            deckId: DeckId,
451→        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
452→            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
453→            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
454→        }
455→    }
456→}

 11→ *  PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.

 13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
 14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 15→ */
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs
 18→
 19→import android.app.Dialog
 20→import android.content.ActivityNotFoundException
 21→import android.content.Intent
 22→import android.os.Bundle
 23→import android.os.Parcelable
 24→import androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultLauncher
 25→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
 26→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 27→import androidx.core.os.BundleCompat
 28→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 29→import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiActivity
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 33→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 34→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.TEXT_PLAIN
 36→import com.ichi2.utils.title
 37→import kotlinx.parcelize.Parcelize
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@NeedsTest("Selecting APKG does not allow multiple files")
 41→@NeedsTest("Selecting COLPKG does not allow multiple files")
 42→@NeedsTest("Restore backup dialog does not allow multiple files")
 43→class ImportFileSelectionFragment : DialogFragment() {
 44→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
 45→        val entries = buildImportEntries()
 46→        return AlertDialog
 47→            .Builder(requireActivity())
 48→            .title(R.string.menu_import)
 49→            .setItems(
 50→                entries.map { requireActivity().getString(it.titleRes) }.toTypedArray(),
 51→            ) { _, position ->
 52→                val entry = entries[position]
 53→                UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 54→                    UsageAnalytics.Category.LINK_CLICKED,
 55→                    entry.analyticsId,
 56→                )
 57→                openImportFilePicker(
 58→                    activity = requireActivity() as AnkiActivity,
 59→                    fileType = entry.type,
 60→                    multiple = entry.multiple,
 61→                    mimeType = entry.mimeType,
 62→                    extraMimes = entry.extraMimes,
 63→                )
 64→            }.create()
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    private fun buildImportEntries(): List<ImportEntry> {
 68→        return arguments?.let { args ->
 69→            args.classLoader = this@ImportFileSelectionFragment::class.java.classLoader
 70→            val options =
 71→                BundleCompat.getParcelable(args, ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS, ImportOptions::class.java)
 72→                    ?: return emptyList()
 73→            mutableListOf<ImportEntry>().apply {
 74→                if (options.importApkg) {
 75→                    add(
 76→                        ImportEntry(
 77→                            R.string.import_deck_package,
 78→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_APKG_FILE,
 79→                            ImportFileType.APKG,
 80→                        ),
 81→                    )
 82→                }
 83→                if (options.importColpkg) {
 84→                    add(
 85→                        ImportEntry(
 86→                            R.string.import_collection_package,
 87→                            UsageAnalytics.Actions.IMPORT_COLPKG_FILE,
 88→                            ImportFileType.COLPKG,
 89→                        ),
 90→                    )
 91→                }
 92→                if (options.importTextFile) {
 93→                    add(
 94→                        ImportEntry(
 95→                            R.string.import_csv,

113→
114→    // safe as this data class is used as a container and it's not involved in any comparing
115→    @Suppress("ArrayInDataClass")
116→    private data class ImportEntry(
117→        @StringRes val titleRes: Int,
118→        val analyticsId: String,
119→        val type: ImportFileType,
120→        var multiple: Boolean = false,
121→        val mimeType: String = "*/*",
122→        val extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
123→    )
124→
125→    @Parcelize
126→    data class ImportOptions(
127→        val importColpkg: Boolean,
128→        val importApkg: Boolean,
129→        val importTextFile: Boolean,
130→    ) : Parcelable
131→
132→    enum class ImportFileType {
133→        APKG,
134→        COLPKG,
135→        CSV,
136→    }
137→
138→    interface ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider {
139→        fun getApkgFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
140→    }
141→
142→    interface CsvImportResultLauncherProvider {
143→        fun getCsvFileImportResultLauncher(): ActivityResultLauncher<Intent>
144→    }
145→
146→    companion object {
147→        private const val ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS = "arg_import_options"
148→
149→        fun newInstance(options: ImportOptions) =
150→            ImportFileSelectionFragment().apply {
151→                arguments = bundleOf(ARG_IMPORT_OPTIONS to options)
152→            }
153→
154→        /**
155→         * Calls through the system with an [Intent] to pick a file to be imported.
156→         */
157→        fun openImportFilePicker(
158→            activity: AnkiActivity,
159→            fileType: ImportFileType,
160→            multiple: Boolean = false,
161→            mimeType: String = "*/*",
162→            extraMimes: Array<String>? = null,
163→        ) {
164→            Timber.d("openImportFilePicker() delegating to file picker intent")
165→            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT)
166→            intent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_OPENABLE)
167→            intent.type = mimeType
168→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_ADVANCED", true)
169→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.FANCY", true)
170→            intent.putExtra("android.content.extra.SHOW_FILESIZE", true)
171→            intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_ALLOW_MULTIPLE, multiple)
172→            extraMimes?.let { intent.putExtra(Intent.EXTRA_MIME_TYPES, it) }
173→
174→            try {
175→                if (
176→                    (fileType == ImportFileType.APKG || fileType == ImportFileType.COLPKG) &&
177→                    activity is ApkgImportResultLauncherProvider
178→                ) {
179→                    activity.getApkgFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
180→                } else if (fileType == ImportFileType.CSV && activity is CsvImportResultLauncherProvider) {
181→                    activity.getCsvFileImportResultLauncher().launch(intent)
182→                } else {
183→                    Timber.w("Activity($activity) can't handle requested import: $fileType")
184→                }
185→            } catch (ex: ActivityNotFoundException) {
186→                Timber.w("No activity to handle openImportFilePicker request")
187→                activity.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→}

I can see the issue. Looking at the code:

1. In `AndroidManifest.xml`, the MIME type intent filter (lines 229-241) only includes `text/comma-separated-values` but NOT `text/csv`
2. In `IntentHandler.kt`, the `getLaunchType` function (lines 336-337) only checks for `text/tab-separated-values` and `text/comma-separated-values`, but NOT `text/csv`

The problem is that **`text/csv`** is a very common MIME type for CSV files (and is used by many file managers on Android), but it's not being recognized by AnkiDroid.

[thinking: Now I understand the complete picture. The `resolveMimeType()` function tries to:
1. First use the intent's type if available
2. If not, determine the MIME type from the file extension

The issue is t...]
Now I understand the complete picture. The issue is that **`text/csv`** (the official IANA MIME type for CSV files) is missing from:

1. The Android manifest intent filter - meaning AnkiDroid won't appear as an option to open CSV files from file managers
2. The `getLaunchType` function in `IntentHandler.kt` - meaning even if the intent reaches AnkiDroid, it won't be properly routed to the text import handler

Let me fix both issues:

[thinking: All changes look correct. Let me provide a summary of the fix....]
All changes have been successfully applied. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Problem
Users couldn't see or open CSV files in AnkiDroid because the app was not handling the `text/csv` MIME type. This is the official IANA MIME type for CSV files (per RFC 4180), and many file managers on Android report CSV files with this type.

## Root Cause
AnkiDroid only supported:
- `text/comma-separated-values` (an older/alternative MIME type)
- `text/tab-separated-values` (for TSV files)

But **NOT** `text/csv`, which is the most commonly used MIME type for CSV files.

## Changes Made

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` (line 241)
Added `text/csv` to the MIME type intent filter so Android will show AnkiDroid as an option when users try to open CSV files from file managers:
```xml
<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>
```

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt` (line 338)
Added `text/csv` to the `getLaunchType` function so CSV files with this MIME type are correctly routed to the text import handler:
```kotlin
mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
    mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" ||
    mimeType == "text/csv" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
```

### 3. `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandlerTest.kt` (lines 99, 104)
Added test cases for `text/csv` to ensure the fix is properly covered:
```kotlin
testIntentType("content://valid", "text/csv", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT)
// ...
testIntentType("content://valid", "text/csv", LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT, Intent.ACTION_SEND)
```

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.171 (commit: 7a65160e).

## Issue to Resolve

Regression: can't select deck in add screen
Also, I can't change decks in the note editor


https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/69634269/91e7b6db-ef3e-4048-b9dd-30545b4405ce

_Originally posted by @BrayanDSO in https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/14171#issuecomment-1697548993_

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 91→        }
 92→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 93→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 94→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 95→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 96→
 97→                // If this item is selected
 98→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
 99→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
100→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
101→                }
102→
103→                // Return the modified view
104→                return tv
105→            }
106→        }
107→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
108→        setSpinnerListener()
109→    }
110→
111→    /**
112→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
113→     */
114→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
115→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
116→    }
117→
118→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
119→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
120→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
121→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
122→            }
123→            true
124→        }
125→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
126→    }
127→
128→    /**
129→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
130→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
131→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
132→     */
133→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
134→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
135→        if (position != -1) {
136→            spinner.setSelection(position)
137→        } else {
138→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
139→        }
140→    }
141→
142→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
143→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
144→    }
145→
146→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
147→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
148→    }
149→
150→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
151→        spinner.visibility = view
152→    }
153→
154→    /**
155→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
156→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
157→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
158→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
159→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
160→     * the current deck id of Collection.
161→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
162→     */
163→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
164→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
165→            selectAllDecks()
166→        } else {
167→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
168→        }
169→    }
170→
171→    /**
172→     * select in the spinner deck with id
173→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
174→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
175→     * @return whether it was found
176→     */
177→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
178→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
179→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
180→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
181→                spinner.setSelection(position)
182→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
183→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
184→                }
185→                return true
186→            }
187→        }
188→        return false
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
193→     * @return whether selection was a success.
194→     */
195→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
196→        if (!showAllDecks) {
197→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
198→            return false
199→        }
200→        spinner.setSelection(0)
201→        return true
202→    }
203→
204→    /**
205→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
206→     */
207→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
208→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
209→        if (showAllDecks) {
210→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
211→        }
212→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
213→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
214→    }
215→
216→    companion object {
217→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
218→    }
219→}

[thinking: Now I think I understand what's going on. Let me look at the `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner` method more closely. I noticed something in `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt`:

```kotlin
fun initializeNoteEdito...]
I found the issue! Looking at the `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner` method, I can see that `mAllDeckIds` is created but never populated with the deck IDs. Let me compare the two initialization methods:

In `initializeActionBarDeckSpinner`:
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val thisDid = d.id
    mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)  // <-- Deck IDs are added
}
```

In `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner`:
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    deckNames.add(currentName)  // <-- Only deck names are added, NO deck IDs!
}
```

The `mAllDeckIds` array is never populated in the Note Editor initialization, which means methods like `selectDeck`, `selectDeckById`, and `updateDeckPosition` won't work properly since they rely on `mAllDeckIds` to find deck positions.

Let me fix this by adding the deck IDs to `mAllDeckIds` in the `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner` method:

 16→package com.ichi2.anki
 17→
 18→import android.view.MotionEvent
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import android.widget.ArrayAdapter
 22→import android.widget.Spinner
 23→import android.widget.TextView
 24→import androidx.appcompat.app.ActionBar
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DeckSelectionDialog.SelectableDeck.Companion.fromCollection
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.widgets.DeckDropDownAdapter
 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.*
 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.Collection
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.FragmentManagerSupplier
 32→import com.ichi2.utils.asFragmentManagerSupplier
 33→import timber.log.Timber
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Handles expansion of a [Spinner], allowing a press to display a [DeckSelectionDialog]
 37→ * Responsible for filtering the decks to display in the [DeckSelectionDialog]
 38→ *
 39→ * Populates the spinner with data, and handles display of the selected deck within the spinner control
 40→ *
 41→ * @param spinner
 42→ * The spinner displayed in the activity.
 43→ * Empty at construction. After initialization, it contains in this order:
 44→ * 1. "All decks" if [showAllDecks] is true
 45→ * 2. All decks from [mAllDeckIds].
 46→ * @param showAllDecks Whether the deck selection should allow "All Decks" as an option
 47→ * @param alwaysShowDefault If true, never hide the default deck. If false, match [DeckPicker]'s logic
 48→ * @param showFilteredDecks whether to show filtered decks
 49→ */
 50→class DeckSpinnerSelection(
 51→    private val context: AnkiActivity,
 52→    private val collection: Collection,
 53→    private val spinner: Spinner,
 54→    private val showAllDecks: Boolean,
 55→    private val alwaysShowDefault: Boolean,
 56→    private val showFilteredDecks: Boolean
 57→) {
 58→    /**
 59→     * All of the decks shown to the user.
 60→     */
 61→    private lateinit var mAllDeckIds: ArrayList<Long>
 62→
 63→    private val mFragmentManagerSupplier: FragmentManagerSupplier = context.asFragmentManagerSupplier()
 64→
 65→    lateinit var dropDownDecks: List<DeckNameId>
 66→        private set
 67→    private var mDeckDropDownAdapter: DeckDropDownAdapter? = null
 68→
 69→    fun initializeActionBarDeckSpinner(actionBar: ActionBar) {
 70→        actionBar.setDisplayShowTitleEnabled(false)
 71→
 72→        // Add drop-down menu to select deck to action bar.
 73→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = showFilteredDecks)
 74→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 75→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 76→            val thisDid = d.id
 77→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 78→        }
 79→        mDeckDropDownAdapter = DeckDropDownAdapter(context, dropDownDecks)
 80→        spinner.adapter = mDeckDropDownAdapter
 81→        setSpinnerListener()
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    fun initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner() {
 85→        dropDownDecks = computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered = false)
 86→        val deckNames = ArrayList<String>(dropDownDecks.size)
 87→        mAllDeckIds = ArrayList(dropDownDecks.size)
 88→        for (d in dropDownDecks) {
 89→            val currentName = d.name
 90→            val thisDid = d.id
 91→            deckNames.add(currentName)
 92→            mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)
 93→        }
 94→        val noteDeckAdapter: ArrayAdapter<String?> = object : ArrayAdapter<String?>(context, R.layout.multiline_spinner_item, deckNames as List<String?>) {
 95→            override fun getDropDownView(position: Int, convertView: View?, parent: ViewGroup): View {
 96→                // Cast the drop down items (popup items) as text view
 97→                val tv = super.getDropDownView(position, convertView, parent) as TextView
 98→
 99→                // If this item is selected
100→                if (position == spinner.selectedItemPosition) {
101→                    tv.setBackgroundColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_background))
102→                    tv.setTextColor(context.getColor(R.color.note_editor_selected_item_text))
103→                }
104→
105→                // Return the modified view
106→                return tv
107→            }
108→        }
109→        spinner.adapter = noteDeckAdapter
110→        setSpinnerListener()
111→    }
112→
113→    /**
114→     * @return All decks, except maybe default if it should be hidden.
115→     */
116→    fun computeDropDownDecks(includeFiltered: Boolean): List<DeckNameId> {
117→        return collection.decks.allNamesAndIds(skipEmptyDefault = true, includeFiltered = includeFiltered)
118→    }
119→
120→    fun setSpinnerListener() {
121→        spinner.setOnTouchListener { _: View?, motionEvent: MotionEvent ->
122→            if (motionEvent.action == MotionEvent.ACTION_UP) {
123→                displayDeckSelectionDialog(collection)
124→            }
125→            true
126→        }
127→        setSpinnerVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Move the selected deck in the spinner to [deckId].
132→     * Timber if [deckId] is not an id of a known deck.
133→     * @param deckId The ID of the deck to select
134→     */
135→    fun updateDeckPosition(deckId: DeckId) {
136→        val position = mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)
137→        if (position != -1) {
138→            spinner.setSelection(position)
139→        } else {
140→            Timber.w("updateDeckPosition() error :: deckId=%d, position=%d", deckId, position)
141→        }
142→    }
143→
144→    fun notifyDataSetChanged() {
145→        mDeckDropDownAdapter!!.notifyDataSetChanged()
146→    }
147→
148→    fun setEnabledActionBarSpinner(enabled: Boolean) {
149→        spinner.isEnabled = enabled
150→    }
151→
152→    fun setSpinnerVisibility(view: Int) {
153→        spinner.visibility = view
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Iterates the drop down decks, and selects the one matching the given id.
158→     * @param deckId The deck id to be selected.
159→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck If true, deckId will be set as the current deck id of Collection
160→     * (this means the deck selected here will continue to appear in any future Activity whose
161→     * display data is loaded from Collection's current deck). If false, deckId will not be set as
162→     * the current deck id of Collection.
163→     * @return True if selection succeeded.
164→     */
165→    fun selectDeckById(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
166→        return if (deckId == ALL_DECKS_ID) {
167→            selectAllDecks()
168→        } else {
169→            selectDeck(deckId, setAsCurrentDeck)
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    /**
174→     * select in the spinner deck with id
175→     * @param deckId The deck id to search (not ALL_DECKS_ID)
176→     * @param setAsCurrentDeck whether this deck should be selected in the collection (if it exists)
177→     * @return whether it was found
178→     */
179→    private fun selectDeck(deckId: DeckId, setAsCurrentDeck: Boolean): Boolean {
180→        for (dropDownDeckIdx in mAllDeckIds.indices) {
181→            if (mAllDeckIds[dropDownDeckIdx] == deckId) {
182→                val position = if (showAllDecks) dropDownDeckIdx + 1 else dropDownDeckIdx
183→                spinner.setSelection(position)
184→                if (setAsCurrentDeck) {
185→                    collection.decks.select(deckId)
186→                }
187→                return true
188→            }
189→        }
190→        return false
191→    }
192→
193→    /**
194→     * Select all decks. Must be called only if [showAllDecks].
195→     * @return whether selection was a success.
196→     */
197→    fun selectAllDecks(): Boolean {
198→        if (!showAllDecks) {
199→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport("selectAllDecks was called while `showAllDecks is false`", "DeckSpinnerSelection:selectAllDecks")
200→            return false
201→        }
202→        spinner.setSelection(0)
203→        return true
204→    }
205→
206→    /**
207→     * Displays a [DeckSelectionDialog]
208→     */
209→    fun displayDeckSelectionDialog(col: Collection?) {
210→        val decks = fromCollection(col!!, includeFiltered = false).toMutableList()
211→        if (showAllDecks) {
212→            decks.add(SelectableDeck(ALL_DECKS_ID, context.resources.getString(R.string.card_browser_all_decks)))
213→        }
214→        val dialog = DeckSelectionDialog.newInstance(context.getString(R.string.search_deck), null, false, decks)
215→        AnkiActivity.showDialogFragment(mFragmentManagerSupplier.getFragmentManager(), dialog)
216→    }
217→
218→    companion object {
219→        const val ALL_DECKS_ID = 0L
220→    }
221→}
222→

The bug was specifically in `initializeNoteEditorDec...]
The fix is complete. Let me summarize what I found and fixed:

## Issue Analysis

The bug was in `DeckSpinnerSelection.kt` in the `initializeNoteEditorDeckSpinner()` method. The `mAllDeckIds` ArrayList was being created with the correct capacity but **never populated** with actual deck IDs.

### Before (buggy code):
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    deckNames.add(currentName)
    // mAllDeckIds was never populated!
}
```

### After (fixed code):
```kotlin
for (d in dropDownDecks) {
    val currentName = d.name
    val thisDid = d.id
    deckNames.add(currentName)
    mAllDeckIds.add(thisDid)  // Now deck IDs are properly added
}
```

## Root Cause

The `mAllDeckIds` array is used by multiple methods that depend on it:
- `updateDeckPosition(deckId)` - uses `mAllDeckIds.indexOf(deckId)` to find the spinner position
- `selectDeck(deckId, ...)` - iterates through `mAllDeckIds.indices` to find and select decks
- `selectDeckById(deckId, ...)` - delegates to `selectDeck()`

When `mAllDeckIds` was empty, all these methods would fail:
- `indexOf()` would always return -1
- The `for` loop in `selectDeck` would never execute

This meant users couldn't select or change decks in the Note Editor, which matches the reported issue exactly.

## Fix Verification

The fix follows the same pattern used in `initializeActionBarDeckSpinner()` which works correctly for the Card Browser. The change is minimal and targeted, only adding the missing line to populate deck IDs.

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.net.Uri
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.CookieManager
 23→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
 24→import android.webkit.WebResourceError
 25→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 26→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 27→import android.webkit.WebView
 28→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
 31→import androidx.annotation.LayoutRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat
 34→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsControllerCompat
 35→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 36→import androidx.lifecycle.flowWithLifecycle
 37→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.ViewerResourceHandler
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.TtsVoicesDialogFragment
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.localizedErrorMessage
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.pages.AnkiServer
 43→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 44→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 45→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val resourceHandler = ViewerResourceHandler(requireContext(), AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return resourceHandler.shouldInterceptRequest(request)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

 60→@UiThread

 62→open class AnkiActivity : AppCompatActivity, SimpleMessageDialogListener {
 63→
 64→    /** The name of the parent class (example: 'Reviewer')  */
 65→    private val activityName: String
 66→    val dialogHandler = DialogHandler(this)
 67→
 68→    private val customTabActivityHelper: CustomTabActivityHelper = CustomTabActivityHelper()
 69→
 70→    constructor() : super() {
 71→        activityName = javaClass.simpleName
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    constructor(@LayoutRes contentLayoutId: Int) : super(contentLayoutId) {
 75→        activityName = javaClass.simpleName
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
 79→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 80→        // The hardware buttons should control the music volume
 81→        volumeControlStream = AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC
 82→        // Set the theme
 83→        Themes.setTheme(this)
 84→        Themes.disableXiaomiForceDarkMode(this)
 85→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 86→        // Disable the notifications bar if running under the test monkey.
 87→        if (AdaptionUtil.isUserATestClient) {
 88→            window.setFlags(
 89→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN,
 90→                WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_FULLSCREEN
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O_MR1) {
 94→            window.navigationBarColor = getColor(R.color.transparent)
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    override fun onStart() {
 99→        super.onStart()
100→        customTabActivityHelper.bindCustomTabsService(this)
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun onStop() {
104→        super.onStop()
105→        customTabActivityHelper.unbindCustomTabsService(this)
106→    }

109→        super.onResume()
110→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(this)
111→        (getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager).cancel(
112→            SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID
113→        )
114→        // Show any pending dialogs which were stored persistently
115→        dialogHandler.executeMessage()
116→    }

118→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
119→        if (item.itemId == android.R.id.home) {
120→            Timber.i("Home button pressed")
121→            return onActionBarBackPressed()
122→        }
123→        return super.onOptionsItemSelected(item)
124→    }

146→    /**

150→     *
151→     * @see .animationEnabled
152→     */
153→    fun animationDisabled(): Boolean {
154→        val preferences = this.sharedPrefs()
155→        return preferences.getBoolean("safeDisplay", false)
156→    }
157→
158→    /**
159→     * Whether animations should be displayed
160→     * This is used to improve the UX for e-ink devices
161→     * Can be tested via Settings - Advanced - Safe display mode
162→     *
163→     * @see .animationDisabled
164→     */
165→    fun animationEnabled(): Boolean {
166→        return !animationDisabled()
167→    }

169→    override fun setContentView(view: View) {
170→        if (animationDisabled()) {
171→            view.clearAnimation()
172→        }
173→        super.setContentView(view)
174→    }
175→
176→    override fun setContentView(view: View, params: ViewGroup.LayoutParams) {
177→        if (animationDisabled()) {
178→            view.clearAnimation()
179→        }
180→        super.setContentView(view, params)
181→    }
182→

184→        if (animationDisabled()) {
185→            view.clearAnimation()

187→        super.addContentView(view, params)

190→    override fun startActivity(intent: Intent) {
191→        startActivityWithAnimation(intent, DEFAULT)
192→    }
193→
194→    fun startActivityWithoutAnimation(intent: Intent) {
195→        disableIntentAnimation(intent)
196→        super.startActivity(intent)
197→        disableActivityAnimation()
198→    }
199→
200→    fun startActivityWithAnimation(
201→        intent: Intent,
202→        animation: Direction
203→    ) {
204→        enableIntentAnimation(intent)
205→        super.startActivity(intent)
206→        enableActivityAnimation(animation)

209→    private fun launchActivityForResult(
210→        intent: Intent?,
211→        launcher: ActivityResultLauncher<Intent?>,
212→        animation: Direction?
213→    ) {
214→        try {
215→            launcher.launch(
216→                intent,
217→                ActivityTransitionAnimation.getAnimationOptions(this, animation)
218→            )
219→        } catch (e: ActivityNotFoundException) {

221→            this.showSnackbar(R.string.activity_start_failed)

231→        super.finish()
232→        enableActivityAnimation(animation)

235→    @Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
236→    protected fun disableViewAnimation(view: View) {

238→    }
239→

241→        if (animationDisabled()) {
242→            disableViewAnimation(view)

244→            view.animation = animation

257→    private fun enableIntentAnimation(intent: Intent) {

264→        if (animationDisabled()) {

271→    /** Method for loading the collection which is inherited by every [AnkiActivity]  */
272→    fun startLoadingCollection() {
273→        Timber.d("AnkiActivity.startLoadingCollection()")
274→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
275→            Timber.d("Synchronously calling onCollectionLoaded")
276→            onCollectionLoaded(getColUnsafe)
277→            return
278→        }
279→        // Open collection asynchronously if it hasn't already been opened
280→        showProgressBar()
281→        CollectionLoader.load(
282→            this
283→        ) { col: Collection? ->
284→            if (col != null) {
285→                Timber.d("Asynchronously calling onCollectionLoaded")
286→                onCollectionLoaded(col)
287→            } else {
288→                onCollectionLoadError()
289→            }
290→        }
291→    }

294→    protected fun onCollectionLoadError() {
295→        val deckPicker = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
296→        deckPicker.putExtra("collectionLoadError", true) // don't currently do anything with this
297→        deckPicker.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
298→        startActivity(deckPicker)
299→    }

301→    fun showProgressBar() {
302→        val progressBar = findViewById<ProgressBar>(R.id.progress_bar)
303→        if (progressBar != null) {
304→            progressBar.visibility = View.VISIBLE
305→        }
306→    }

309→        val progressBar = findViewById<ProgressBar>(R.id.progress_bar)
310→        if (progressBar != null) {
311→            progressBar.visibility = View.GONE
312→        }
313→    }

315→    internal fun mayOpenUrl(url: Uri) {
316→        val success = customTabActivityHelper.mayLaunchUrl(url, null, null)
317→        if (!success) {
318→            Timber.w("Couldn't preload url: %s", url.toString())
319→        }

322→    @KotlinCleanup("toast -> snackbar")
323→    open fun openUrl(url: Uri) {
324→        if (!AdaptionUtil.hasWebBrowser(this)) {
325→            showSnackbar(getString(R.string.no_browser_msg, url.toString()))
326→            return
327→        }
328→        val toolbarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
329→        val navBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(this, R.attr.customTabNavBarColor, 0)
330→        val colorSchemeParams = CustomTabColorSchemeParams.Builder()
331→            .setToolbarColor(toolbarColor)
332→            .setNavigationBarColor(navBarColor)
333→            .build()
334→        val builder = CustomTabsIntent.Builder(customTabActivityHelper.session)
335→            .setShowTitle(true)
336→            .setStartAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_right_in, R.anim.slide_left_out)
337→            .setExitAnimations(this, R.anim.slide_left_in, R.anim.slide_right_out)
338→            .setCloseButtonIcon(
339→                BitmapFactory.decodeResource(
340→                    this.resources,
341→                    R.drawable.ic_back_arrow_custom_tab
342→                )
343→            )
344→            .setColorScheme(customTabsColorScheme)
345→            .setDefaultColorSchemeParams(colorSchemeParams)
346→        val customTabsIntent = builder.build()
347→        CustomTabsHelper.addKeepAliveExtra(this, customTabsIntent.intent)
348→        CustomTabActivityHelper.openCustomTab(this, customTabsIntent, url, CustomTabsFallback())
349→    }
350→
351→    fun openUrl(urlString: String) {
352→        openUrl(Uri.parse(urlString))
353→    }
354→
355→    fun openUrl(@StringRes url: Int) {
356→        openUrl(getString(url))
357→    }
358→
359→    private val customTabsColorScheme: Int
360→        get() = if (AppCompatDelegate.getDefaultNightMode() == AppCompatDelegate.MODE_NIGHT_FOLLOW_SYSTEM) {
361→            COLOR_SCHEME_SYSTEM
362→        } else if (Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode) {
363→            COLOR_SCHEME_DARK
364→        } else {
365→            COLOR_SCHEME_LIGHT
366→        }
367→
368→    /**
369→     * Global method to show dialog fragment including adding it to back stack Note: DO NOT call this from an async
370→     * task! If you need to show a dialog from an async task, use showAsyncDialogFragment()
371→     *
372→     * @param newFragment  the DialogFragment you want to show
373→     */
374→    open fun showDialogFragment(newFragment: DialogFragment) {
375→        runOnUiThread {
376→            showDialogFragment(this, newFragment)
377→        }
378→    }
379→
380→    /**
381→     * Calls [.showAsyncDialogFragment] internally, using the channel
382→     * [Channel.GENERAL]
383→     *
384→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
385→     */
386→    open fun showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment) {
387→        showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment, Channel.GENERAL)
388→    }
389→
390→    /**
391→     * Global method to show a dialog fragment including adding it to back stack and handling the case where the dialog
392→     * is shown from an async task, by showing the message in the notification bar if the activity was stopped before the
393→     * AsyncTask completed
394→     *
395→     * @param newFragment  the AsyncDialogFragment you want to show
396→     * @param channel the Channel to use for the notification
397→     */
398→    fun showAsyncDialogFragment(
399→        newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment,
400→        channel: Channel
401→    ) {
402→        try {
403→            showDialogFragment(newFragment)
404→        } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
405→            Timber.w("failed to show fragment, activity is likely paused. Sending notification")
406→            // Store a persistent message to SharedPreferences instructing AnkiDroid to show dialog
407→            DialogHandler.storeMessage(newFragment.dialogHandlerMessage?.toMessage())
408→            // Show a basic notification to the user in the notification bar in the meantime
409→            val title = newFragment.notificationTitle
410→            val message = newFragment.notificationMessage
411→            showSimpleNotification(title, message, channel)
412→        }
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Show a simple message dialog, dismissing the message without taking any further action when OK button is pressed.
417→     * If a DialogFragment cannot be shown due to the Activity being stopped then the message is shown in the
418→     * notification bar instead.
419→     *
420→     * @param message
421→     * @param reload flag which forces app to be restarted when true
422→     */
423→    open fun showSimpleMessageDialog(
424→        message: String,
425→        title: String = "",
426→        reload: Boolean = false
427→    ) {
428→        val newFragment: AsyncDialogFragment =
429→            SimpleMessageDialog.newInstance(title, message, reload)
430→        showAsyncDialogFragment(newFragment)
431→    }
432→
433→    fun showSimpleNotification(
434→        title: String,
435→        message: String?,
436→        channel: Channel
437→    ) {
438→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
439→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
440→        if (prefs.getString(MINIMUM_CARDS_DUE_FOR_NOTIFICATION, "0")!!
441→            .toInt() <= Preferences.PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
442→        ) {
443→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
444→            val ticker: String? = if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
445→                message
446→            } else {
447→                title
448→            }
449→            // Build basic notification
450→            val builder = NotificationCompat.Builder(
451→                this,
452→                channel.id
453→            )
454→                .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
455→                .setContentTitle(title)
456→                .setContentText(message)
457→                .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
458→                .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
459→                .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
460→                .setTicker(ticker)
461→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
462→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
463→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
464→            }
465→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
466→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
467→            }
468→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
469→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
470→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
471→            val resultPendingIntent = PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
472→                this,
473→                0,
474→                resultIntent,
475→                PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
476→                false
477→            )
478→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
479→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
480→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
481→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    // Handle closing simple message dialog
486→    override fun dismissSimpleMessageDialog(reload: Boolean) {
487→        dismissAllDialogFragments()
488→        if (reload) {
489→            val deckPicker = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
490→            deckPicker.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
491→            startActivity(deckPicker)
492→        }
493→    }

495→    // Dismiss whatever dialog is showing

500→        )
501→    }
502→
503→    /**

507→     */
508→    protected fun enableToolbar(): ActionBar {

510→            ?: // likely missing "<include layout="@layout/toolbar" />"
511→            throw IllegalStateException("Unable to find toolbar")

513→        return supportActionBar!!
514→    }
515→
516→    /**
517→     * sets [.getSupportActionBar] and returns the action bar
518→     * @param view the view which contains a toolbar element:

521→     */
522→    protected fun enableToolbar(view: View): ActionBar {

530→    protected fun showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) =
531→        showedActivityFailedScreen(
532→            savedInstanceState = savedInstanceState,
533→            activitySuperOnCreate = { state -> super.onCreate(state) }
534→        )
535→
536→    /** @see Window.setNavigationBarColor */
537→    fun setNavigationBarColor(@AttrRes attr: Int) {
538→        window.navigationBarColor = ThemeUtils.getThemeAttrColor(this, attr)
539→    }

541→    fun closeCollectionAndFinish() {
542→        Timber.i("closeCollectionAndFinish()")
543→        CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("AnkiActivity:closeCollectionAndFinish()")
544→        finish()
545→    }

547→    /**
548→     * If storage permissions are not granted, shows a toast message and finishes the activity.
549→     *
550→     * This should be called AFTER a call to `super.`[onCreate]
551→     *
552→     * @return `true`: activity may continue to start, `false`: [onCreate] should stop executing
553→     * as storage permissions are mot granted
554→     */
555→    fun ensureStoragePermissions(): Boolean {
556→        if (IntentHandler.grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {

564→    companion object {
565→        const val DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG = "dialog"
566→
567→        /** Extra key to set the finish animation of an activity  */
568→        const val FINISH_ANIMATION_EXTRA = "finishAnimation"
569→
570→        fun showDialogFragment(activity: AnkiActivity, newFragment: DialogFragment) {
571→            showDialogFragment(activity.supportFragmentManager, newFragment)
572→        }
573→
574→        fun showDialogFragment(manager: FragmentManager, newFragment: DialogFragment) {
575→            // DialogFragment.show() will take care of adding the fragment
576→            // in a transaction. We also want to remove any currently showing
577→            // dialog, so make our own transaction and take care of that here.
578→            val ft = manager.beginTransaction()
579→            val prev = manager.findFragmentByTag(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
580→            if (prev != null) {
581→                ft.remove(prev)
582→            }
583→            // save transaction to the back stack
584→            ft.addToBackStack(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
585→            newFragment.show(ft, DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
586→            manager.executePendingTransactions()
587→        }

589→        private const val SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID = 0
590→    }

18→import android.content.Context

22→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
23→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView

25→import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
26→import com.ichi2.utils.getInstanceFromClassName
27→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
28→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
29→
30→/**
31→ * Activity aimed to host a fragment on the entire screen.
32→ * For that, it uses [R.layout.single_fragment_activity], which has only a [FragmentContainerView]
33→ *
34→ * Useful to avoid creating a Activity for every new screen
35→ * while being able to reuse the fragment on other places.
36→ *
37→ * [getIntent] can be used as an easy way to build a [SingleFragmentActivity]
38→ */
39→open class SingleFragmentActivity : AnkiActivity() {
40→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
41→        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
42→            return
43→        }
44→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
45→        setContentView(R.layout.single_fragment_activity)
46→        setTransparentStatusBar()
47→
48→        // avoid recreating the fragment on configuration changes
49→        if (savedInstanceState != null) {
50→            return
51→        }
52→
53→        val fragmentClassName = requireNotNull(intent.getStringExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA)) {
54→            "'$FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA' extra should be provided"
55→        }
56→        val fragment = getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName).apply {
57→            arguments = intent.getBundleExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA)
58→        }
59→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
60→            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment)
61→        }
62→    }
63→
64→    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
65→        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
66→        if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
67→            return true
68→        }
69→        return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
70→    }
71→
72→    companion object {
73→        const val FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA = "fragmentName"
74→        const val FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA = "fragmentArgs"
75→
76→        fun getIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out Fragment>, arguments: Bundle? = null): Intent {
77→            return Intent(context, SingleFragmentActivity::class.java).apply {
78→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
79→                putExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA, arguments)
80→            }
81→        }
82→    }
83→}
84→
85→interface DispatchKeyEventListener {
86→    fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean
87→}
