 29→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromPreference
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 31→import java.util.*
 32→import java.util.function.BiFunction
 33→import java.util.stream.Collectors
 34→
 35→/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 36→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 37→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 38→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 42→    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 43→    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 44→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 45→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 46→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 47→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 48→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 49→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 50→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 51→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 52→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 53→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 59→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 60→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 61→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 62→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 63→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 64→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 65→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 66→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 67→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 68→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 69→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 70→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 71→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 72→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 73→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 74→
 75→    companion object {
 76→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 77→            get() = Arrays.stream(values())
 78→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 79→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    val preferenceKey: String
 83→        get() = "binding_$name"
 84→
 85→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 86→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 87→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 88→            collection.remove(element)
 89→            collection.add(0, element)
 90→            true
 91→        }
 92→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 96→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 97→            // do not reorder the elements
 98→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
 99→                return@BiFunction false
100→            }
101→            collection.add(element)
102→            return@BiFunction true
103→        }
104→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
108→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
109→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
110→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
111→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
112→    }
113→
114→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
115→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
116→        bindings.remove(binding)
117→        prefs.edit {
118→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
123→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
124→        get() = // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
125→            when (this) {
126→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> from(
127→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
128→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
129→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
130→                )
131→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> from(
132→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
133→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
134→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
135→                )
136→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> from(
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
139→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
140→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> from(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
149→                )
150→                EDIT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
151→                MARK -> from(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
152→                BURY_CARD -> from(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
153→                BURY_NOTE -> from(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
154→                SUSPEND_CARD -> from(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
155→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> from(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
156→                PLAY_MEDIA -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
157→                REPLAY_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
158→                RECORD_VOICE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
159→                UNDO -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
161→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
162→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
163→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
164→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
165→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
166→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
167→                SHOW_HINT -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
168→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
169→                ADD_NOTE -> from(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                else -> ArrayList()
171→            }
172→
173→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, keys: ModifierKeys): MappableBinding {
174→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
178→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun from(vararg bindings: MappableBinding): List<MappableBinding> {
182→        return ArrayList(listOf(*bindings))
183→    }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keyCode: Int, side: CardSide): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keyCode), MappableBinding.Screen.Reviewer(side))
187→    }
188→
189→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
190→        /**
191→         *
192→         * example failure: answering an ease on the front of the card
193→         */
194→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
195→    }
196→}

17→package com.ichi2.anki
18→
19→import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
20→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
21→import com.google.android.material.snackbar.Snackbar
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.TR
23→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.undo
25→import com.ichi2.libanki.undoableOp
26→
27→/** If there's an action pending in the review queue, undo it and show a snackbar */
28→suspend fun FragmentActivity.undoAndShowSnackbar(duration: Int = Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT) {
29→    withProgress {
30→        val changes = undoableOp {
31→            if (!undoAvailable()) {
32→                OpChangesAfterUndo.getDefaultInstance()
33→            } else {
34→                undo()
35→            }
36→        }
37→        val message = if (changes.operation.isEmpty()) {
38→            TR.actionsNothingToUndo()
39→        } else {
40→            TR.undoActionUndone(changes.operation)
41→        }
42→        showSnackbar(message, duration)
43→    }
44→}
45→

17→package com.ichi2.libanki
18→
19→import anki.collection.OpChangesAfterUndo
20→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
21→import anki.collection.UndoStatus as UndoStatusProto
22→
23→/**
24→ * If undo/redo available, a localized string describing the action will be set.
25→ */
26→data class UndoStatus(
27→    val undo: String?,
28→    val redo: String?,
29→    // not currently used
30→    val lastStep: Int
31→) {
32→    companion object {
33→        fun from(proto: UndoStatusProto): UndoStatus {
34→            return UndoStatus(
35→                undo = proto.undo.ifEmpty { null },
36→                redo = proto.redo.ifEmpty { null },
37→                lastStep = proto.lastStep
38→            )
39→        }
40→    }
41→}
42→
43→/**
44→ * Undo the last backend operation.
45→ *
46→ * Should be called via collection.op(), which will notify
47→ * [ChangeManager.Subscriber] of the changes.
48→ *
49→ * Will throw if no undo operation is possible (due to legacy code
50→ * directly mutating the database).
51→ */
52→@RustCleanup("Once fully migrated, and v2 scheduler dropped, rename to undo()")
53→fun Collection.undo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
54→    return backend.undo()
55→}
56→
57→/** Redoes the previously-undone operation. See the docs for
58→[Collection.undoOperation]
59→ */
60→fun Collection.redo(): OpChangesAfterUndo {
61→    return backend.redo()
62→}
63→
64→/** See [UndoStatus] */
65→fun Collection.undoStatus(): UndoStatus {
66→    return UndoStatus.from(backend.getUndoStatus())
67→}
68→
69→fun Collection.redoAvailable(): Boolean {
70→    val status = undoStatus()
71→    return status.redo != null
72→}
73→
74→/** eg "Redo suspend card" if redo available */
75→fun Collection.redoLabel(): String? {
76→    val action = undoStatus().redo
77→    return action?.let { tr.undoRedoAction(it) }
78→}
79→

The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Change...]
The implementation of the Redo feature is now complete. Here's a summary of all the changes made:

## Summary of Changes

### New Files Created
1. **AnkiDroid/src/main/res/drawable/ic_redo.xml** - Redo icon (mirrored version of undo icon)

### Modified Files

1. **ViewerCommand.kt** - Added `REDO` enum value with Shift+Z as default keyboard binding

2. **BackendUndo.kt** - Added `redoAvailable()` and `redoLabel()` functions

3. **Undo.kt** - Added `redoAndShowSnackbar()` suspend function

4. **AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt** - Added `redo()` function and `ViewerCommand.REDO` handling

5. **Reviewer.kt** - Added:
   - `R.id.action_redo` handling in `onOptionsItemSelected()`
   - Redo button setup in `onCreateOptionsMenu()` (enabled/disabled state, icon alpha, title)

6. **ActionButtonStatus.kt** - Added setup for customizable redo button

7. **CustomButtonsSettingsFragment.kt** - Added redo to reset settings function

8. **reviewer.xml** - Changed redo button default to `showAsAction="ifRoom"`

9. **ReviewerKeyboardInputTest.kt** - Added redo mock to test class

## Features Implemented
- **Redo Button**: Appears in the reviewer toolbar (shows as action if room available)
- **Keyboard Shortcut**: Shift+Z to redo (consistent with desktop Anki)
- **Customizable**: Users can customize the redo button position in Settings > Controls > App bar buttons
- **Feedback**: Shows a snackbar message after redo action (e.g., "Action redone")
- **State Management**: Button is enabled/disabled based on redo availability
- **Dynamic Title**: Button title updates to show what action will be redone (e.g., "Redo Answer Card")

The implementation leverages the existing backend support for redo (already present via `backend.redo()`) and follows the established patterns used by the undo functionality.

 65→
 66→open class AnkiDroidJsAPI(
 67→    private val activity: AbstractFlashcardViewer,
 68→) {
 69→    private val currentCard: Card
 70→        get() = activity.currentCard!!
 71→
 72→    private val getColUnsafe: Collection
 73→        get() = activity.getColUnsafe
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     Javascript Interface class for calling Java function from AnkiDroid WebView
 77→     see js-api.js for available functions
 78→     */
 79→
 80→    private val context: Context = activity
 81→
 82→    // Text to speech
 83→    private val talker = JavaScriptTTS()
 84→
 85→    // Speech to Text
 86→    private val speechRecognizer = JavaScriptSTT(context)
 87→
 88→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 89→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 90→        boolean: Boolean,
 91→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Boolean(apiContract.isValid, boolean).toString().toByteArray()
 92→
 93→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 94→        apiContract: ApiContract,
 95→        int: Int,
 96→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Integer(apiContract.isValid, int).toString().toByteArray()
 97→
 98→    open fun convertToByteArray(
 99→        apiContract: ApiContract,
100→        long: Long,
101→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.Long(apiContract.isValid, long).toString().toByteArray()
102→
103→    open fun convertToByteArray(
104→        apiContract: ApiContract,
105→        string: String,
106→    ): ByteArray = ApiResult.String(apiContract.isValid, string).toString().toByteArray()
107→
108→    /**
109→     * The method parse json data and return api contract object
110→     * @param byteArray
111→     * @return apiContract or null
112→     */
113→    private fun parseJsApiContract(byteArray: ByteArray): ApiContract? {
114→        try {
115→            val data = JSONObject(byteArray.decodeToString())
116→            val cardSuppliedApiVersion = data.optString("version", "")
117→            val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact = data.optString("developer", "")
118→            val cardSuppliedData = data.optString("data", "")
119→            val isValid = requireApiVersion(cardSuppliedApiVersion, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
120→            return ApiContract(isValid, cardSuppliedDeveloperContact, cardSuppliedData)
121→        } catch (j: JSONException) {
122→            Timber.w(j)
123→            activity.runOnUiThread {
124→                activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, j.localizedMessage))
125→            }
126→        }
127→        return null
128→    }
129→
130→    /*
131→     * see 02-strings.xml
132→     * Show Error code when mark card or flag card unsupported
133→     * 1 - mark card
134→     * 2 - flag card
135→     *
136→     * show developer contact if js api used in card is deprecated
137→     */
138→    private fun showDeveloperContact(
139→        errorCode: Int,
140→        apiDevContact: String,
141→    ) {
142→        val errorMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.anki_js_error_code, errorCode)
143→        val snackbarMsg: String = context.getString(R.string.api_version_developer_contact, apiDevContact, errorMsg)
144→
145→        activity.showSnackbar(snackbarMsg, Snackbar.LENGTH_INDEFINITE) {
146→            setMaxLines(3)
147→            setAction(R.string.reviewer_invalid_api_version_visit_documentation) {
148→                activity.openUrl(Uri.parse("https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki"))
149→            }
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    /**
154→     * Supplied api version must be equal to current api version to call mark card, toggle flag functions etc.
155→     */
156→    private fun requireApiVersion(
157→        apiVer: String,
158→        apiDevContact: String,
159→    ): Boolean {
160→        try {
161→            if (apiDevContact.isEmpty() || apiVer.isEmpty()) {
162→                activity.runOnUiThread {
163→                    activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.invalid_json_data, ""))
164→                }
165→                return false
166→            }
167→            val versionCurrent = Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.CURRENT_JS_API_VERSION)
168→            val versionSupplied = Version.parse(apiVer)
169→
170→            /*
171→             * if api major version equals to supplied major version then return true and also check for minor version and patch version
172→             * show toast for update and contact developer if need updates
173→             * otherwise return false
174→             */
175→            return when {
176→                versionSupplied == versionCurrent -> {
177→                    true
178→                }
179→                versionSupplied.isLowerThan(versionCurrent) -> {
180→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
181→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.update_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
182→                    }
183→                    versionSupplied.isHigherThanOrEquivalentTo(Version.parse(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.MINIMUM_JS_API_VERSION))
184→                }
185→                else -> {
186→                    activity.runOnUiThread {
187→                        activity.showSnackbar(context.getString(R.string.valid_js_api_version, apiDevContact))
188→                    }
189→                    false
190→                }
191→            }
192→        } catch (e: Exception) {
193→            Timber.w(e, "requireApiVersion::exception")
194→        }
195→        return false
196→    }
197→
198→    /**
199→     * Handle js api request,
200→     * some of the methods are overridden in Reviewer.kt and default values are returned.
201→     * @param methodName
202→     * @param bytes
203→     * @param returnDefaultValues `true` if default values should be returned (if non-[Reviewer])
204→     * @return
205→     */
206→    open suspend fun handleJsApiRequest(
207→        methodName: String,
208→        bytes: ByteArray,
209→        returnDefaultValues: Boolean = true,
210→    ) = withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
211→        // the method will call to set the card supplied data and is valid version for each api request
212→        val apiContract = parseJsApiContract(bytes)!!
213→        // if api not init or is api not called from reviewer then return default -1
214→        // also other action will not be modified
215→        if (!apiContract.isValid or returnDefaultValues) {
216→            return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, -1)
217→        }
218→
219→        val cardDataForJsAPI = activity.getCardDataForJsApi()
220→        val apiParams = apiContract.cardSuppliedData
221→
222→        return@withContext when (methodName) {
223→            "init" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
224→            "newCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.newCardCount)
225→            "lrnCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.lrnCardCount)
226→            "revCardCount" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.revCardCount)
227→            "eta" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.eta)
228→            "nextTime1" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime1)
229→            "nextTime2" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime2)
230→            "nextTime3" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime3)
231→            "nextTime4" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, cardDataForJsAPI.nextTime4)
232→            "toggleFlag" -> {
233→                if (apiParams !in flagCommands) {
234→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_FLAG_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
235→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
236→                }
237→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.executeCommand(flagCommands[apiParams]!!))
238→            }
239→            "markCard" ->
240→                processAction({
241→                    activity.executeCommand(ViewerCommand.MARK)
242→                }, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_MARK_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
243→            "buryCard" -> processAction(activity::buryCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURY_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
244→            "buryNote" -> processAction(activity::buryNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_BURT_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
245→            "suspendCard" -> processAction(activity::suspendCard, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_CARD, ::convertToByteArray)
246→            "suspendNote" -> processAction(activity::suspendNote, apiContract, ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SUSPEND_NOTE, ::convertToByteArray)
247→            "setCardDue" -> {
248→                try {
249→                    val days = apiParams.toInt()
250→                    if (days < 0 || days > 9999) {
251→                        showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
252→                        return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
253→                    }
254→                    activity.launchCatchingTask {
255→                        activity.rescheduleCards(listOf(currentCard.id), days)
256→                    }
257→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
258→                } catch (e: NumberFormatException) {
259→                    showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SET_DUE, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
260→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
261→                }
262→            }
263→            "resetProgress" -> {
264→                val cardIds = listOf(currentCard.id)
265→                activity.launchCatchingTask { activity.resetCards(cardIds) }
266→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
267→            }
268→            "cardMark" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.note(getColUnsafe).hasTag(getColUnsafe, "marked"))
269→            "cardFlag" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.userFlag().code)
270→            "cardReps" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.reps)
271→            "cardInterval" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.ivl)
272→            "cardFactor" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.factor)
273→            "cardMod" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.mod)
274→            "cardId" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.id)
275→            "cardNid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.nid)
276→            "cardType" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.type.code)
277→            "cardDid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.did)
278→            "cardLeft" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.left)
279→            "cardODid" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDid)
280→            "cardODue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.oDue)
281→            "cardQueue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.queue.code)
282→            "cardLapses" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.lapses)
283→            "cardDue" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, currentCard.due)
284→            "deckName" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, Decks.basename(activity.getColUnsafe.decks.name(currentCard.did)))
285→            "isActiveNetworkMetered" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, NetworkUtils.isActiveNetworkMetered())
286→            "ttsSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setLanguage(apiParams))
287→            "ttsSpeak" -> {
288→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
289→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
290→                val queueMode = jsonObject.getInt("queueMode")
291→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.speak(text, queueMode))
292→            }
293→            "ttsIsSpeaking" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.isSpeaking)
294→            "ttsSetPitch" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setPitch(apiParams.toFloat()))
295→            "ttsSetSpeechRate" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.setSpeechRate(apiParams.toFloat()))
296→            "ttsFieldModifierIsAvailable" -> {
297→                // Know if {{tts}} is supported - issue #10443
298→                // Return false for now
299→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
300→            }
301→            "ttsStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, talker.stop())
302→            "searchCard" -> {
303→                val intent =
304→                    Intent(context, CardBrowser::class.java).apply {
305→                        putExtra("currentCard", currentCard.id)
306→                        putExtra("search_query", apiParams)
307→                    }
308→                activity.startActivity(intent)
309→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
310→            }
311→            "searchCardWithCallback" -> ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract)
312→            "isDisplayingAnswer" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isDisplayingAnswer)
313→            "addTagToCard" -> {
314→                activity.runOnUiThread { activity.showTagsDialog() }
315→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
316→            }
317→            "isInFullscreen" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isFullscreen)
318→            "isTopbarShown" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.prefShowTopbar)
319→            "isInNightMode" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, activity.isInNightMode)
320→            "enableHorizontalScrollbar" -> {
321→                activity.webView!!.isHorizontalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
322→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
323→            }
324→            "enableVerticalScrollbar" -> {
325→                activity.webView!!.isVerticalScrollBarEnabled = apiParams.toBoolean()
326→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
327→            }
328→            "showNavigationDrawer" -> {
329→                activity.onNavigationPressed()
330→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
331→            }
332→            "showOptionsMenu" -> {
333→                activity.openOptionsMenu()
334→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
335→            }
336→            "showToast" -> {
337→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
338→                val text = jsonObject.getString("text")
339→                val shortLength = jsonObject.optBoolean("shortLength", true)
340→                val msgDecode = activity.decodeUrl(text)
341→                showThemedToast(context, msgDecode, shortLength)
342→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
343→            }
344→            "showAnswer" -> {
345→                activity.displayCardAnswer()
346→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
347→            }
348→            "answerEase1" -> {
349→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.AGAIN)
350→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
351→            }
352→            "answerEase2" -> {
353→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.HARD)
354→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
355→            }
356→            "answerEase3" -> {
357→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.GOOD)
358→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
359→            }
360→            "answerEase4" -> {
361→                activity.flipOrAnswerCard(Ease.EASY)
362→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
363→            }
364→
365→            "addTagToNote" -> {
366→                val jsonObject = JSONObject(apiParams)
367→                val noteId = jsonObject.getLong("noteId")
368→                val tag = jsonObject.getString("tag")
369→                val note =
370→                    getColUnsafe.getNote(noteId).apply {
371→                        addTag(tag)
372→                    }
373→                getColUnsafe.updateNote(note)
374→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
375→            }
376→            "sttSetLanguage" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.setLanguage(apiParams))
377→            "sttStart" -> {
378→                val callback =
379→                    object : JavaScriptSTT.SpeechRecognitionCallback {
380→                        override fun onResult(results: List<String>) {
381→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
382→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.success(Json.encodeToString(ListSerializer(String.serializer()), results))
383→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
384→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
385→                            }
386→                        }
387→
388→                        override fun onError(errorMessage: String) {
389→                            activity.lifecycleScope.launch {
390→                                val apiResult = ApiResult.failure(errorMessage)
391→                                val jsonEncodedString = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) { JSONObject.quote(apiResult.toString()) }
392→                                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSttResult($jsonEncodedString)", null)
393→                            }
394→                        }
395→                    }
396→                speechRecognizer.setRecognitionCallback(callback)
397→                convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.start())
398→            }
399→            "sttStop" -> convertToByteArray(apiContract, speechRecognizer.stop())
400→            else -> {
401→                showDeveloperContact(ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_ERROR, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
402→                throw Exception("unhandled request: $methodName")
403→            }
404→        }
405→    }
406→
407→    private fun processAction(
408→        action: () -> Boolean,
409→        apiContract: ApiContract,
410→        errorCode: Int,
411→        conversion: (ApiContract, Boolean) -> ByteArray,
412→    ): ByteArray {
413→        val status = action()
414→        if (!status) {
415→            showDeveloperContact(errorCode, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
416→        }
417→        return conversion(apiContract, status)
418→    }
419→
420→    @NeedsTest("needs coverage")
421→    private suspend fun ankiSearchCardWithCallback(apiContract: ApiContract): ByteArray =
422→        withContext(Dispatchers.Main) {
423→            val cards =
424→                try {
425→                    searchForRows(apiContract.cardSuppliedData, SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering(), CardsOrNotes.CARDS)
426→                        .map { withCol { getCard(it.cardOrNoteId) } }
427→                } catch (exc: Exception) {
428→                    activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript(
429→                        "console.log('${context.getString(R.string.search_card_js_api_no_results)}')",
430→                        null,
431→                    )
432→                    showDeveloperContact(AnkiDroidJsAPIConstants.ANKI_JS_ERROR_CODE_SEARCH_CARD, apiContract.cardSuppliedDeveloperContact)
433→                    return@withContext convertToByteArray(apiContract, false)
434→                }
435→            val searchResult: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
436→            for (card in cards) {
437→                val jsonObject = JSONObject()
438→                val fieldsData = card.note(getColUnsafe).fields
439→                val fieldsName = card.noteType(getColUnsafe).fieldsNames
440→
441→                val noteId = card.nid
442→                val cardId = card.id
443→                jsonObject.put("cardId", cardId)
444→                jsonObject.put("noteId", noteId)
445→
446→                val jsonFieldObject = JSONObject()
447→                fieldsName.zip(fieldsData).forEach { pair ->
448→                    jsonFieldObject.put(pair.component1(), pair.component2())
449→                }
450→                jsonObject.put("fieldsData", jsonFieldObject)
451→
452→                searchResult.add(jsonObject.toString())
453→            }
454→
455→            // quote result to prevent JSON injection attack
456→            val jsonEncodedString = JSONObject.quote(searchResult.toString())
457→            activity.runOnUiThread {
458→                activity.webView!!.evaluateJavascript("ankiSearchCard($jsonEncodedString)", null)
459→            }
460→            convertToByteArray(apiContract, true)
461→        }
462→
463→    open class CardDataForJsApi {
464→        var newCardCount: Int = -1
465→        var lrnCardCount: Int = -1
466→        var revCardCount: Int = -1
467→        var eta = -1
468→        var nextTime1 = ""
469→        var nextTime2 = ""
470→        var nextTime3 = ""
471→        var nextTime4 = ""
472→    }
473→
474→    sealed class ApiResult protected constructor(
475→        private val status: JvmBoolean,
476→    ) {
477→        class Boolean(
478→            status: JvmBoolean,
479→            val value: JvmBoolean,
480→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
481→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
482→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
483→            }
484→        }
485→
486→        class Integer(
487→            status: JvmBoolean,
488→            val value: JvmInt,
489→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
490→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
491→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
492→            }
493→        }
494→
495→        class Float(
496→            status: JvmBoolean,
497→            val value: JvmFloat,
498→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
499→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
500→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
501→            }
502→        }
503→
504→        class Long(
505→            status: JvmBoolean,
506→            val value: JvmLong,
507→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
508→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
509→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
510→            }
511→        }
512→
513→        class String(
514→            status: JvmBoolean,
515→            val value: JvmString,
516→        ) : ApiResult(status) {
517→            override fun putValue(o: JSONObject) {
518→                o.put(VALUE_KEY, value)
519→            }
520→        }
521→
522→        abstract fun putValue(o: JSONObject)
523→
524→        override fun toString() =
525→            JSONObject()
526→                .apply {
527→                    put(SUCCESS_KEY, status)
528→                    putValue(this)
529→                }.toString()
530→
531→        @Suppress("RemoveRedundantQualifierName") // we don't want `String(true, value)`
532→        companion object {
533→            fun success(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(true, value)
534→
535→            fun failure(value: JvmString) = ApiResult.String(false, value)
536→        }
537→    }
538→
539→    class ApiContract(
540→        val isValid: Boolean,
541→        val cardSuppliedDeveloperContact: String,
542→        val cardSuppliedData: String,
543→    )
544→
545→    companion object {
546→        /**
547→         * Key for a success value.
548→         */
549→        const val VALUE_KEY = "value"
550→        const val SUCCESS_KEY = "success"
551→    }
552→}

42→package com.ichi2.utils
43→
44→import org.json.JSONArray
45→import org.json.JSONObject
46→
47→fun JSONArray.deepClone(): JSONArray {
48→    val clone = JSONArray()
49→    for (i in 0 until length()) {
50→        clone.put(
51→            when (get(i)) {
52→                is JSONObject -> getJSONObject(i).deepClone()
53→                is JSONArray -> getJSONArray(i).deepClone()
54→                else -> get(i)
55→            },
56→        )
57→    }
58→    return clone
59→}
60→
61→fun JSONArray.jsonObjectIterable(): Iterable<JSONObject> = Iterable { jsonObjectIterator() }
62→
63→@KotlinCleanup("see if jsonObject/string/longIterator() methods can be combined into one")
64→fun JSONArray.jsonObjectIterator(): Iterator<JSONObject> =
65→    object : Iterator<JSONObject> {
66→        private var index = 0
67→
68→        override fun hasNext(): Boolean = index < length()
69→
70→        override fun next() =
71→            getJSONObject(index).also {
72→                index++
73→            }
74→    }
75→
76→fun JSONArray.stringIterable(): Iterable<String> = Iterable { stringIterator() }
77→
78→fun JSONArray.stringIterator(): Iterator<String> {
79→    return object : Iterator<String> {
80→        private var index = 0
81→
82→        override fun hasNext(): Boolean = index < length()
83→
84→        override fun next(): String {
85→            val string = getString(index)
86→            index++
87→            return string
88→        }
89→    }
90→}
91→
92→/**
93→ * @return Given an array of objects, return the array of the value with `key`, assuming that they are String.
94→ * E.g. templates, fields are a JSONArray whose objects have name
95→ */
96→fun JSONArray.toStringList(key: String): List<String> = jsonObjectIterable().map { it.getString(key) }

 68→            ntid: NoteTypeId,
 69→        ): Note {
 70→            val backendNote = col.backend.newNote(ntid)
 71→            return Note(col, backendNote)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    fun load(col: Collection) {
 76→        val note = col.backend.getNote(id)
 77→        loadFromBackendNote(col, note)
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    @LibAnkiAlias("_load_from_backend_note")
 81→    private fun loadFromBackendNote(
 82→        col: Collection,
 83→        note: anki.notes.Note,
 84→    ) {
 85→        id = note.id
 86→        guId = note.guid
 87→        mid = note.notetypeId
 88→        notetype = col.notetypes.get(noteTypeId)!! // not in libAnki
 89→        mod = note.mtimeSecs
 90→        usn = note.usn
 91→        // the lists in the protobuf are NOT mutable, even though they cast to MutableList
 92→        tags = note.tagsList.toMutableList()
 93→        fields = note.fieldsList.toMutableList()
 94→        fMap = Notetypes.fieldMap(notetype)
 95→    }

 97→    @NotInLibAnki
 98→    fun numberOfCards(col: Collection): Int = cardIds(col).size
 99→
100→    fun cardIds(col: Collection): List<Long> = col.cardIdsOfNote(nid = id)
101→
102→    fun cards(col: Collection): List<Card> = cardIds(col).map { col.getCard(it) }
103→
104→    @LibAnkiAlias("ephemeral_card")
105→    fun ephemeralCard(
106→        col: Collection,
107→        ord: Int = 0,
108→        customNoteType: NotetypeJson? = null,
109→        customTemplate: CardTemplate? = null,
110→        fillEmpty: Boolean = false,
111→        deckId: DeckId = DEFAULT_DECK_ID,
112→    ): Card {
113→        val card = Card(col, id = null)
114→        card.ord = ord
115→        card.did = deckId
116→
117→        val model = customNoteType ?: notetype
118→        val template =
119→            if (customTemplate != null) {
120→                customTemplate.deepClone()
121→            } else {
122→                val index = if (model.isStd) ord else 0
123→                model.tmpls[index]
124→            }
125→        // may differ in cloze case
126→        template.setOrd(card.ord)
127→
128→        val output =
129→            TemplateManager.TemplateRenderContext
130→                .fromCardLayout(
131→                    note = this,
132→                    card = card,
133→                    notetype = model,
134→                    template = template,
135→                    fillEmpty = fillEmpty,
136→                ).render(col)
137→        card.renderOutput = output
138→        card.note = this
139→        return card
140→    }
141→
142→    /** The first card, assuming it exists. */
143→    @CheckResult
144→    @NotInLibAnki
145→    fun firstCard(col: Collection): Card =
146→        col.getCard(
147→            col.db.queryLongScalar(
148→                "SELECT id FROM cards WHERE nid = ? ORDER BY ord LIMIT 1",
149→                id,
150→            ),
151→        )

153→    /**
154→     * Dict interface
155→     * ***********************************************************
156→     */
157→    fun keys(): Array<String> = fMap!!.keys.toTypedArray()
158→
159→    @KotlinCleanup("see if we can make this immutable")
160→    fun values(): MutableList<String> = fields
161→
162→    fun items(): Array<Array<String>> {
163→        // TODO: Revisit this method. The field order returned differs from Anki.
164→        // The items here are only used in the note editor, so it's a low priority.
165→        val result =
166→            Array(
167→                fMap!!.size,
168→            ) { emptyStringArray(2) }
169→        for (fname in fMap!!.keys) {
170→            val i = fMap!![fname]!!.first
171→            result[i][0] = fname
172→            result[i][1] = fields[i]
173→        }
174→        return result
175→    }
176→
177→    @LibAnkiAlias("_field_index")
178→    private fun fieldIndex(key: String): Int {
179→        val fieldPair =
180→            fMap!![key]
181→                ?: throw IllegalArgumentException(
182→                    String.format(
183→                        "No field named '%s' found",
184→                        key,
185→                    ),
186→                )
187→        return fieldPair.first
188→    }
189→
190→    @LibAnkiAlias("__getitem__")
191→    fun getItem(key: String): String = fields[fieldIndex(key)]
192→
193→    @LibAnkiAlias("__setitem__")
194→    fun setItem(
195→        key: String,
196→        value: String,
197→    ) {
198→        fields[fieldIndex(key)] = value
199→    }
200→
201→    @LibAnkiAlias("__contains__")
202→    operator fun contains(key: String): Boolean = fMap!!.containsKey(key)
203→
204→    @NotInLibAnki
205→    fun setField(
206→        index: Int,
207→        value: String,
208→    ) {
209→        fields[index] = value
210→    }
211→
212→    /**
213→     * Tags
214→     * ***********************************************************
215→     */
216→
217→    @LibAnkiAlias("has_tag")
218→    fun hasTag(
219→        col: Collection,
220→        tag: String,
221→    ): Boolean = col.tags.inList(tag, tags)
222→
223→    /**
224→     * Add tag. Duplicates will be stripped on save.
225→     */
226→    @LibAnkiAlias("add_tag")
227→    fun addTag(tag: String) {
228→        tags.add(tag)
229→    }
230→
231→    @LibAnkiAlias("remove_tag")
232→    fun removeTag(tag: String) {
233→        val rem: MutableList<String> =
234→            ArrayList(
235→                tags.size,
236→            )
237→        for (t in tags) {
238→            if (t.equals(tag, ignoreCase = true)) {
239→                rem.add(t)
240→            }
241→        }
242→        for (r in rem) {
243→            tags.remove(r)
244→        }
245→    }
246→
247→    @LibAnkiAlias("string_tags")
248→    fun stringTags(col: Collection): String = col.tags.join(col.tags.canonify(tags))
249→
250→    @LibAnkiAlias("set_tags_from_str")
251→    fun setTagsFromStr(
252→        col: Collection,
253→        str: String,
254→    ) {
255→        tags = col.tags.split(str)
256→    }
257→
258→    /**
259→     * Unique/duplicate check
260→     * ***********************************************************
261→     */
262→
263→    @LibAnkiAlias("fields_check")
264→    fun fieldsCheck(col: Collection): NoteFieldsCheckResponse.State = col.backend.noteFieldsCheck(toBackendNote()).state
265→
266→    public override fun clone(): Note =
267→        try {
268→            super.clone() as Note
269→        } catch (e: CloneNotSupportedException) {
270→            throw RuntimeException(e)
271→        }
272→
273→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
274→        if (this === other) return true
275→        if (other == null || javaClass != other.javaClass) return false
276→        val note = other as Note
277→        return id == note.id
278→    }
279→
280→    override fun hashCode(): Int = (id xor (id ushr 32)).toInt()
281→
282→    object ClozeUtils {
283→        private val clozeRegexPattern = Pattern.compile("\\{\\{c(\\d+)::")
284→
285→        /**
286→         * Calculate the next number that should be used if inserting a new cloze deletion.
287→         * Per the manual the next number should be greater than any existing cloze deletion
288→         * even if there are gaps in the sequence, and regardless of existing cloze ordering
289→         *
290→         * @param fieldValues Iterable of field values that may contain existing cloze deletions
291→         * @return the next index that a cloze should be inserted at
292→         */
293→        @KotlinCleanup("general regex fixes for '.group' being nullable")
294→        fun getNextClozeIndex(fieldValues: Iterable<String>): Int {
295→            var highestClozeId = 0
296→            // Begin looping through the fields
297→            for (fieldLiteral in fieldValues) {
298→                // Begin searching in the current field for cloze references
299→                val matcher = clozeRegexPattern.matcher(fieldLiteral)
300→                while (matcher.find()) {
301→                    val detectedClozeId = matcher.group(1)!!.toInt()
302→                    if (detectedClozeId > highestClozeId) {
303→                        highestClozeId = detectedClozeId
304→                    }
305→                }
306→            }
307→            return highestClozeId + 1
308→        }
309→    }
310→}
311→

[thinking: All lint checks pass. Let me summarize what I've implemented:

1. **AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt**:
   - Added import for `JSONArray` and `stringIterable`
   - Added `setNoteTags` endpoint that takes `noteId` an...]
I have successfully implemented the feature request to add JS API functions for managing note tags. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. AnkiDroidJsAPI.kt
- Added imports for `JSONArray` and `stringIterable`
- Added two new API endpoints:
  - **`setNoteTags`**: Takes `noteId` and `tags` (array of strings) and replaces all tags on the note
  - **`getNoteTags`**: Takes `noteId` and returns the tags as a JSON array

### 2. js-api.js
- Added `ankiSetNoteTags` and `ankiGetNoteTags` to the `jsApiList`
- Added special handlers for both methods with proper parameter handling:
  - `ankiSetNoteTags(noteId, tags)` - accepts noteId and an array of tag strings
  - `ankiGetNoteTags(noteId)` - accepts noteId and returns a JSON array of tags

### 3. AnkiDroidJsAPITest.kt
- Added three unit tests:
  - **`ankiGetNoteTagsTest`**: Tests retrieving tags from a note
  - **`ankiSetNoteTagsTest`**: Tests setting/replacing tags on a note  
  - **`ankiRemoveTagBySettingTagsTest`**: Tests the full workflow of removing a tag (get → filter → set)

## Usage Example

To **remove a tag** using the new API (as requested in the issue):

```javascript
const api = AnkiDroidJS.init({ developer: "dev@example.com", version: "0.0.3" });

// Get current note ID
const noteIdResult = await api.ankiGetCardNid();
const noteId = noteIdResult.value;

// Get current tags
const tagsResult = await api.ankiGetNoteTags(noteId);
const currentTags = JSON.parse(tagsResult.value);

// Remove a specific tag
const filteredTags = currentTags.filter(tag => tag !== "tagToRemove");

// Set the updated tags
await api.ankiSetNoteTags(noteId, filteredTags);
```

This REST-style API design (as discussed in the issue) allows users to:
- Get tags
- Add tags (by appending to the array)
- Remove tags (by filtering and setting)
- Replace all tags (by setting a new array)

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

 23→/**
 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}
 57→
 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}
 74→
 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}
 93→
 94→fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95→    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)

103→fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(

14→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
15→
16→import android.content.Context
17→import android.content.Intent
18→import android.os.Bundle
19→import android.webkit.WebView
20→import androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback
21→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
22→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
24→import com.ichi2.anki.SingleFragmentActivity
25→import com.ichi2.anki.hideShowButtonCss
26→
27→class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28→    override val title: String
29→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30→    override val pageName: String
31→        get() = "import-page"
32→    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33→    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34→    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35→
36→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37→        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38→        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39→            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40→                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41→                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42→                remove()
43→            }
44→        }
45→        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46→            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47→        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50→    }
51→
52→    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53→        private val path: String,
54→        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55→    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56→        /**
57→         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58→         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59→         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60→         */
61→        private var isDone = false
62→
63→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64→            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65→            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68→            }
69→        }
70→
71→        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72→            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73→            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74→                url == null -> false
75→                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76→                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77→                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78→                    false
79→                }
80→                else -> false
81→            }
82→        }
83→    }
84→
85→    companion object {
86→        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87→
88→        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89→            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90→            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91→        }
92→    }
93→}
94→

 24→/**
 25→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 26→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 27→ * and this routine will return false.
 28→ *
 29→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 30→ *
 31→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 32→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 33→ * to check for success.
 34→ *
 35→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 36→ *
 37→ */
 38→fun Collection.createBackup(
 39→    backupFolder: String,
 40→    force: Boolean,
 41→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 42→): Boolean {
 43→    return backend.createBackup(
 44→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 45→        force = force,
 46→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 47→    )
 48→}
 49→
 50→/**
 51→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 52→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 53→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 54→ */
 55→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 56→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 57→}

 59→/**
 60→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 61→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 62→ * */
 63→fun importCollectionPackage(
 64→    backend: Backend,
 65→    colPath: String,
 66→    colpkgPath: String
 67→) {
 68→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 69→        colPath = colPath,
 70→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 71→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 72→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 73→    )
 74→}

 76→/**
 77→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 78→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 79→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 80→ */
 81→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 82→    outPath: String,
 83→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 84→    legacy: Boolean = true
 85→) {
 86→    close(forFullSync = true)
 87→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 88→        outPath = outPath,
 89→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 90→        legacy = legacy
 91→    )
 92→    reopen()
 93→}

The fix has been successfully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

The issue requested updating AnkiDroid to use the Anki 23.12 backend. The codebase was already at backend version `0.1.34-anki23.12.1` (line 12 in `build.gradle`), but there was a missing API change required to compile with the new backend.

## Changes Made

**File: `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`**

1. Added import for the new options builder:
   ```kotlin
   import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
   ```

2. Updated the `exportAnkiPackage` function to use the new options-based API:
   ```kotlin
   fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
       outPath: String,
       withScheduling: Boolean,
       withMedia: Boolean,
       limit: ExportLimit,
       legacy: Boolean = true
   ) {
       val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
           this.withScheduling = withScheduling
           this.withMedia = withMedia
           this.legacy = legacy
           this.withDeckConfigs = true
       }
       backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
   }
   ```

This change matches the workaround diff provided in the issue discussion. The backend version was already updated to `0.1.34-anki23.12.1`, and now the API call has been updated to use the new `exportAnkiPackageOptions` builder pattern required by the 23.12 backend.

You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.178 (commit: f03d541c).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: display order bug for notes
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Go to "card browser" then "options" and under "toggle cards/notes" select "notes". Go to "change display order" and choose another option.

### Expected behaviour

The display order should stay as set, like when "cards" is selected in "toggle cards/notes."

### Actual behaviour

When you select "notes", it automatically sets the display order to "by created time" oldest to newest. If you choose another option it doesn't change except by pressing "by created time" again to flip the direction.  This only works until you exit the app or add a new note, and it then flips to oldest to newest again.

39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}
78→
